.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/hot-topic-discovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:15:This scenario validates that `runtime query CLI mode='hot_nodes'` materially beats the no-graph baseline for surfacing the most-contested or most-supported topics in a council session — useful for post-deliberation analysis, retrospective synthesis, and identifying where seat conflict concentrates.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/hot-topic-discovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:32:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, identify the top 3 hot nodes in a seeded 4-round council session — first via the no-graph baseline (operator counts cross-references manually), then via runtime query CLI with mode hot_nodes — and confirm the with-graph path returns a ranked list backed by edge-degree counts in one runtime CLI call.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/hot-topic-discovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:45:2. Run **no-graph baseline**: `rg` for each claim/decision id across `ai-council/**`; manually count cross-references; produce ranked estimate.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/hot-topic-discovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:51:`As a council-graph integration validator, identify the top 3 hot nodes in a seeded 4-round council session — first via the no-graph baseline (operator counts cross-references manually), then via runtime query CLI with mode hot_nodes — and confirm the with-graph path returns a ranked list backed by edge-degree counts in one runtime CLI call.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/hot-topic-discovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:57:1. `bash: for id in c1 c2 c3 c4 c5 decA decB; do echo "$id: $(rg -c "$id" <spec-folder>**/*.md 2>/dev/null | wc -l)"; done` (text-level cross-reference estimate)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/hot-topic-discovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:84:| DAC-031 | Hot-topic discovery value | Demonstrate graph ranks by edge-degree; baseline returns text-count estimate | `As a council-graph integration validator, identify the top 3 hot nodes in a seeded 4-round council session — first via the no-graph baseline (operator counts cross-references manually), then via runtime query CLI with mode hot_nodes — and confirm the with-graph path returns a ranked list backed by edge-degree counts in one runtime CLI call.` | baseline: rg counts -> with-graph: upsert + query hot_nodes | Top-3 = {c1, decA, +1} with degree counts | Baseline tally + graph response | PASS if top-3 contains c1 AND decA | Inspect getHotNodes ranking + degree field |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:5:  - "deep-ai-council folder layout"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:6:  - "ai-council artifacts"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:16:Packet-local artifact layout for completed AI Council runs.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:22:**Persistence is mandatory for every council run.** The target packet path is resolved by the agent at §1 Step 0 RESOLVE in `deep-ai-council.md` across all 4 runtime mirrors. When invocation cannot resolve a packet path, the agent HALT-and-ASKs the user for one and does NOT dispatch council seats, so the layout below applies to every completed deliberation.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:24:The `ai-council/` folder is the packet-local artifact home for `@deep-ai-council` runs. It mirrors the familiar shape of `research/` and `review/`: configuration, strategy, append-only state, per-round evidence, and a final synthesized report.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:27:specs/<track>/<NNN-name>/ai-council/
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:28:|-- ai-council-config.json
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:29:|-- ai-council-strategy.md
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:30:|-- ai-council-state.jsonl
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:48:`ai-council-config.json` is one mutable packet config. It tracks the active `spec_folder`, `current_round`, `max_rounds`, `seats_per_round`, `convergence_signal`, timestamps, and status.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:50:`ai-council-strategy.md` is the first-run charter. It records task framing, selected lenses, executor/vantage targets, input evidence, constraints, and the convergence rule.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:52:`ai-council-state.jsonl` is the append-only state log. It records `round_start`, `seat_returned`, `deliberation_synthesized`, `round_end`, `council_complete`, `artifact_written`, `rollback`, and `artifact_superseded` events for resume and audit.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:64:**Canonical writer:** the council writes these artifacts directly with `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/lib/persist-artifacts.cjs` named exports. Each writer is scoped to `ai-council/**` and appends an `artifact_written` audit event. `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs` remains a helper fallback for non-council callers.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:69:- Agent body: `.opencode/agents/ai-council.md` §12 OUTPUT PROTOCOL (this layout) + §13 INVOCATION CONTRACT + §14 STATE SCHEMA + §16 COUNCIL PERSISTENCE PROTOCOL + §18 ROLLBACK FOR OPERATORS
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:70:- Council writer library: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/lib/persist-artifacts.cjs`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:71:- Helper fallback: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:72:- Schema: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/output_schema.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:73:- State format: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:74:- Command wiring: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:75:- Decision records: ADR-002 (folder layout), ADR-004 (validator policy); the helper/schema/§17/parity ADRs; the v1.1 metadata + payload routing + advisor + command wiring ADRs
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:29:The shipped surface is anchored by `runtime upsert CLI`, `runtime status CLI`, `deep-ai-council`. The playbook scenario `08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md` defines the operator prompt, command sequence, expected signals, evidence, and pass/fail criteria for DAC-020.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.2.0.0.md:7:This release aligns `deep-ai-council` with the shared deep-skill resource model while preserving its planning-only domain. It adds a council-specific quick reference, loop protocol, and runtime asset family so operators see the same resource shape as `deep-research` and `deep-review` without copy-pasting their loop vocabulary.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.2.0.0.md:18:- Added `references/quick_reference.md` for commands, artifact map, stop conditions, validation, and sibling-skill routing boundaries.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.2.0.0.md:27:- Added `assets/runtime_capabilities.json` as the runtime parity and validation matrix.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.2.0.0.md:33:- Updated `README.md` version, structure, related documents, asset inventory, and validation navigation.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:3:description: "This scenario validates that `runtime upsert CLI` returns explicit no-op success on empty input rather than erroring. Anchors to council-graph-script.vitest.ts test 'treats empty upsert as an explicit no-op success'."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:15:This scenario validates that calling `runtime upsert CLI` with empty `nodes` and empty `edges` arrays returns explicit no-op success (the response indicates `inserted: 0` or equivalent `noop: true` flag) instead of either erroring or silently swallowing the input.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:29:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, call runtime upsert CLI with empty nodes and empty edges arrays and assert the response indicates explicit no-op success.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:49:`As a council-graph integration validator, call runtime upsert CLI with empty nodes and empty edges arrays and assert the response indicates explicit no-op success.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:76:| DAC-020 | runtime upsert CLI empty input no-op | Verify empty payload returns explicit no-op success | `As a council-graph integration validator, call runtime upsert CLI with empty nodes and empty edges arrays and assert the response indicates explicit no-op success.` | status -> upsert (empty) -> status | Explicit no-op response + unchanged counts | runtime CLI responses + status counts | PASS if no-op success and unchanged counts | Inspect scripts/upsert.cjs empty-input branch |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/lib/README.md:29:The lane scripts deliberately keep three separate `parseArgs` dialect families. A single shared parser is NOT extracted because no behavior-preserving superset exists across all of them, and the 017 review brief requires preferring divergence over a risky behavior-changing refactor.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/lib/README.md:33:| Equals-only, bare flag is boolean | `score-candidate.cjs`, `promote-candidate.cjs`, `generate-profile.cjs`, `scan-integration.cjs`, `rollback-candidate.cjs`, `check-mirror-drift.cjs` | `--key=value` only | A bare `--key` is `true`. All docs and YAML invoke these with the equals form, so adding space-form would change parsing semantics (`--key value` would bind the value instead of staying boolean) for no caller benefit. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.3.0.0.md:1:deep-ai-council's fifteen reference files are now grouped into five topic subfolders, so a reader reaches convergence, scoring, structure, pattern, and integration docs by area instead of scanning one flat list.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.3.0.0.md:11:- **Grouped references/ into convergence/, scoring/, structure/, patterns/, and integration/** -- the fifteen references sat in one flat directory. They now live under five topic subfolders. The smart router discovers references recursively, so routing behavior is unchanged while navigation gets clearer.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.3.0.0.md:12:- **Updated the README structure tree and every inbound link** -- the SKILL.md REFERENCES paths and intent map, the README structure diagram, and the @ai-council agent mirrors across four runtimes resolve to the subfoldered paths. A workspace sweep confirmed zero stale flat reference paths in live consumers.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:1:First release of `deep-ai-council` as a dedicated skill. Council deliberation moves out of `system-spec-kit` and into its own package, the runtime agent is renamed from `multi-ai-council` to `deep-ai-council` across all three runtimes, the advisor learns to route council prompts to the new skill, and the planning-only boundary is enforced through scoped-write helpers that only ever touch packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts. Two spec packets ship behind this release: 101/001 created the skill surface, 101/003 expanded references and tests to fully document the agent contract.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:3:> Spec folders: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/101-deep-multi-ai-council-skill/{001-deep-ai-council-skill-creation, 003-deep-ai-council-reference-expansion}` (Level 1 each)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:9:The headline change is that AI council deliberation now has a real home. Before this release, the council was a multi-strategy planning idea threaded through `system-spec-kit`, but the references, persistence scripts, and routing all lived inside an unrelated skill, which made it hard to evolve council behavior without touching spec-folder governance. This release lifts council out into its own package with its own runtime agent.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:15:The new `deep-ai-council` skill owns council activation triggers, smart routing across eight council intents, the planning-only operational boundary, and the rules that make council reports reproducible. The skill stays codebase-agnostic, so any project can dispatch a council without inheriting Webflow or OpenCode-specific patterns.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:21:The runtime agent now ships as `@deep-ai-council` across all three runtime mirrors (OpenCode, Claude, Codex). The agent body documents the 9-step workflow from RESOLVE through DELIVER, the dispatch depth rules (Depth 0 parallel via `Task`, Depth 1 sequential via `sequential_thinking` MCP), and the persistence protocol that calls writer functions in a fixed order so every artifact lands with an audit event.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:27:Natural prompts like "Run an AI council deliberation to compare implementation plans" used to fall through to a generic explainer because the source scorer's read-only guard tripped on phrases like "compare implementation plans". The advisor source scorer now allows explicit `deep-ai-council` council phrases through that guard, returning the new skill with confidence 0.95 and uncertainty 0.12 on the regression suite.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:33:The release adds a clean library boundary for everything the council writes to disk, and it preserves the additive-only state-event policy so existing council JSONL state stays readable.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:39:A new library at `scripts/lib/persist-artifacts.js` exposes seven named writer functions, one per artifact type (`writeConfig`, `writeStrategyMd`, `writeStateJsonl`, `writeSeat`, `writeDeliberation`, `writeCritique`, `writeReport`). Every writer resolves the destination under `<packet>/ai-council/`, refuses to write outside that root with an `OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE` error, then appends an `artifact_written` audit event with byte count and SHA-256 checksum to the append-only state log. The previous CLI wrapper at `scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs` is now a thin entry point that delegates to the library, so non-council callers still work unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:45:The state log keeps the v1 event types (round_start, seat_returned, deliberation_synthesized, round_end, council_complete) and adds v1.2 audit events (`artifact_written`, `rollback`, `artifact_superseded`) without breaking older readers. The protocol string stays `multi-ai-council` for compatibility with existing council state rows produced before the rename — the policy is additive only, never rewrite history.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:51:Failed rounds move into `ai-council/failed/round-NNN-<timestamp>/` with matching `artifact_superseded` markers in the state log. Operators can inspect the failed round folder for forensic context, then dispatch a fresh round; in-place retry is intentionally out of scope so the audit trail stays clean.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:63:Six references cover the day-to-day surface: `output_schema.md` (the markdown report contract the parser enforces), `folder_layout.md` (packet-local artifact tree), `state_format.md` (append-only JSONL events), `command_wiring.md` (caller-owned post-dispatch persistence), `seat_diversity_patterns.md` (lens combinations and vantage targets), and `convergence_signals.md` (two-of-three-agree rule and escape hatches). Four further references were added in 101/003 to lift agent-body logic into the reference layer: `scoring_rubric.md` documents the 5-dimension rubric, multi-round deliberation, and the Hunter / Skeptic / Referee adversarial cross-critique; `depth_dispatch.md` codifies Depth 0 vs Depth 1 detection and no-recursive-counciling rules; `failure_handling.md` walks through timeouts, all-seats-fail, contradiction-without-resolution, and insufficient vantage diversity; `anti_patterns.md` lists the eleven council anti-patterns with detection cues and recovery actions.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:75:The playbook follows the canonical `manual_testing_playbook.md` plus numbered category folders shape. Eighteen scenarios across seven categories (DAC-001 through DAC-018) cover runtime routing, council deliberation and seat diversity, artifact persistence and state format, convergence and rollback, scope boundaries, depth detection and resume semantics, and the writer library contract. Every per-feature file carries the required nine-column scenario table, realistic operator prompts, and source anchors back to the implementation.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:81:Targeted vitest coverage already exists at `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/multi-ai-council-*.vitest.ts` and continues to pass after the rename, the persistence library refactor, and the sk-code style alignment.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:97:No new top-level commands ship in this release. The agent is dispatched through normal Task delegation patterns, and the persistence helper at `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs` runs as a CLI wrapper for callers that need to persist a captured council report from outside the agent context.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:167:No migration is required. The state schema stays additive-only, so existing `ai-council/` artifacts produced before the rename remain readable. Callers that previously dispatched `@multi-ai-council` should switch to `@deep-ai-council`; no shim is provided because no active runtime consumer of the old name was found at rename time.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:3:description: "Real-world scenario where a council process is interrupted mid-round. Graph status returns counts, readiness, and a namespace-scoped recovery payload; baseline requires manual JSONL parsing. Anchors to `runtime status CLI`."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:15:This scenario validates that `runtime status CLI` materially beats the no-graph baseline for the operations task: "the council process was killed mid-round — where did state stop, and how do I recover?"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:19:The append-only `ai-council-state.jsonl` captures every state transition, including `council_complete`. When the process is interrupted before `council_complete`, the operator must determine the last completed round, what artifacts persisted, and whether to resume or roll back. Without graph: parse the JSONL line-by-line, cross-reference filesystem artifacts. With graph: `runtime status CLI` returns counts + readiness + namespace-scoped `recovery` payload in one call.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:27:Operators run the exact prompt and command sequence for `DAC-032` and confirm the with-graph path produces a structured recovery payload while the no-graph baseline requires manual JSONL forensics.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:29:- Objective: Demonstrate `runtime status CLI` returns structured recovery context where the baseline requires manual JSONL parsing.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:30:- Real-world situation: A 4-round council was killed (e.g., SIGKILL) during round 3. `ai-council-state.jsonl` carries `round_started` for round 3 but no `round_complete` and no `council_complete` event. Graph rows for rounds 1 and 2 are fully persisted; round 3 has partial seat nodes but no DECISION node. Operator returns to triage the interrupted run.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:32:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, recover from an interrupted council session — first via the no-graph baseline (manually parse ai-council-state.jsonl), then via runtime status CLI — and confirm the with-graph path returns counts, readiness, and a namespace-scoped recovery payload in one runtime CLI call.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:33:- Expected execution process: Seed an interrupted state: 2 full rounds persisted to graph, 1 partial round, no `council_complete` event in JSONL. Run baseline JSONL forensics. Run `runtime status CLI`. Compare diagnostic quality.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:34:- Expected signals: Baseline yields raw JSONL events requiring operator interpretation. Graph returns `counts: { sessions, rounds, seats, claims, evidence, disagreements, decisions, recommendations }`, `readiness: ...`, `signals: { ... }`, `recovery: { replay-hint scoped to (specFolder, sessionId) }`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:44:1. Seed: persist artifacts for rounds 1 + 2 fully, then simulate interruption (round 3 partial: ROUND node + 1 SEAT node, no DECISION, no `council_complete` event in JSONL).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:45:2. Run **no-graph baseline**: parse `ai-council-state.jsonl` manually; find last event for the session; determine what artifacts persisted.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:47:4. Compare diagnostic completeness.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:51:`As a council-graph integration validator, recover from an interrupted council session — first via the no-graph baseline (manually parse ai-council-state.jsonl), then via runtime status CLI — and confirm the with-graph path returns counts, readiness, and a namespace-scoped recovery payload in one runtime CLI call.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:58:2. `bash: rg -c 'round_complete|council_complete' <spec-folder>` (count completion events)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:73:Capture: baseline tail/parse transcript + operator hypothesis, graph status response with all 4 fields, one-line value summary ("graph returned structured recovery payload; baseline produced raw JSONL events requiring interpretation").
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:77:- **Pass**: Graph response includes non-empty `recovery` payload scoped to `(sandbox/dac-032, dac-032-run-01)`. `counts` reflects 2 full rounds + 1 partial. `readiness` distinguishes incomplete state from healthy state.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:86:| DAC-032 | Mid-run interruption recovery value | Demonstrate graph status produces recovery payload; baseline needs JSONL forensics | `As a council-graph integration validator, recover from an interrupted council session — first via the no-graph baseline (manually parse ai-council-state.jsonl), then via runtime status CLI — and confirm the with-graph path returns counts, readiness, and a namespace-scoped recovery payload in one runtime CLI call.` | baseline: tail+rg+find -> with-graph: upsert + status | Structured counts + readiness + recovery payload vs raw JSONL | Baseline transcript + graph status response | PASS if non-empty namespace-scoped recovery payload | Inspect status P2-001 + namespace filter |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/mid-run-interruption-recovery-graph-vs-baseline.md:106:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md` | Documents append-only `ai-council-state.jsonl` event contract |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:3:description: "Append-only JSONL event format for AI Council state, resume and audit records."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:5:  - "deep-ai-council state format"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:6:  - "ai-council-state jsonl"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:16:`ai-council-state.jsonl` is append-only JSONL. Each line is one state event used for resume, audit, and convergence decisions.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:22:Every council run MUST close with a `council_complete` event appended after `writeReport(...)` lands `council-report.md`. Runs that exit before `council_complete` are incomplete and should be resumed or rolled back before completion is claimed.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:56:  event: "council_complete";
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:99:  protocol?: "ai-council";
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:107:- `protocol: "ai-council"` distinguishes council state from other JSONL logs.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:120:3. v1 rows without `schema_version`, `protocol`, or `producer` remain valid.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:158:v1.2 adds audit events to the same append-only JSONL. Each artifact write records the relative `ai-council/` path, byte count, sha256 checksum, timestamp, optional seat id, and round id.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:161:{"schema_version":"1.2","protocol":"ai-council","producer":"persist-artifacts@1.2.0","event":"artifact_written","path":"ai-council-state.jsonl","bytes":842,"checksum":"sha256:0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef","timestamp":"2026-05-08T22:30:00.000Z","seat_id":null,"round_id":"round-001"}
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:162:{"schema_version":"1.2","protocol":"ai-council","producer":"persist-artifacts@1.2.0","event":"artifact_written","path":"seats/round-001/seat-001-cli-codex.md","bytes":2048,"checksum":"sha256:aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa","timestamp":"2026-05-08T22:30:01.000Z","seat_id":"seat-001","round_id":"round-001"}
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:163:{"schema_version":"1.2","protocol":"ai-council","producer":"persist-artifacts@1.2.0","event":"rollback","round_id":"round-001","reason":"seat-002 timed out","timestamp":"2026-05-08T22:31:00.000Z","rollback_event_id":"rollback-round-001-20260508T223100Z","supersedes":["seats/round-001/seat-001-cli-codex.md"]}
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:164:{"schema_version":"1.2","protocol":"ai-council","producer":"persist-artifacts@1.2.0","event":"artifact_superseded","original_path":"seats/round-001/seat-001-cli-codex.md","round_id":"round-001","rollback_event_id":"rollback-round-001-20260508T223100Z","superseded_by":"rollback","timestamp":"2026-05-08T22:31:00.000Z"}
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:167:v1 readers must ignore `artifact_written`, `rollback`, and `artifact_superseded` events without error. v1.2 readers use them for completeness summaries, checksum verification, and rollback forensics.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:173:The last completed event determines the next action. If `round_start` exists without all expected `seat_returned` events, redo or complete that round. If all seats returned but `deliberation_synthesized` is missing, synthesize deliberation. If deliberation exists but `round_end` is missing, close the round. If `council_complete` exists, treat the council as done unless the user requests another round.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:179:Per ADR-003, validation is convention-only for v1. Do not add a runtime schema validator unless a follow-on packet proves real drift.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md:182:- Agent body: `.opencode/agents/ai-council.md` §14

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:1:First major iteration of `deep-ai-council` after the initial v1.0.0.0 skill release. This series ships a dedicated council graph as a derived projection (Phase 003), full operator playbook coverage of that graph (Phase 004), 6 fix-ups and value-comparison scenarios proving the graph beats the no-graph baseline (Phase 005), CI-protected fixture-driven value vitest with measured ratios (Phase 006), runnable test-gate infrastructure plus 32-entry feature catalog plus reverse-anchor meta-test plus replay helper plus Codex TOML parity plus fixture provenance (Phase 007), and skill-surface polish plus this changelog plus smoke coverage for the new helper scripts (Phase 008). All seven phases land in 101 as additive child packets; runtime code and authoritative artifact contracts stay unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:3:> Spec folders: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/101-deep-multi-ai-council-skill/{003-deep-ai-council-graph-support, 004-deep-ai-council-playbook-graph-coverage, 005-deep-ai-council-fixups-and-graph-value-scenarios, 006-council-graph-value-scenario-automation, 007-council-infrastructure-hardening, 008-council-surface-polish}` (Level 1-3 across the six phases)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:11:A derived SQLite projection now indexes council deliberation as graph rows. Four MCP tools own the surface — `council_graph_upsert`, `council_graph_query`, `council_graph_status`, `council_graph_convergence` — registered as a distinct family from the existing `deep_loop_graph_*` research/review graph. Eight node kinds (SESSION, ROUND, SEAT, CLAIM, EVIDENCE, DISAGREEMENT, DECISION, RECOMMENDATION), ten relation kinds (PARTICIPATES_IN, PROPOSES, SUPPORTS, CONTRADICTS, DERIVES_FROM, AGREES_WITH, RESOLVES, ESCALATES, EVIDENCE_FOR, RECOMMENDS), five query modes (`unresolved_disagreements`, `evidence_chain`, `decision_support`, `convergence_blockers`, `hot_nodes`), and three convergence buckets (`STOP_ALLOWED`, `CONTINUE`, `STOP_BLOCKED`) cover the full council deliberation lifecycle. ADR-001 documents the dedicated-projection decision over deep-loop graph reuse. Artifacts under `ai-council/**` stay authoritative — the graph rebuilds from them.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:17:The manual testing playbook grew from 18 to 32 scenarios across 9 categories. Phase 004 added 8 functional graph integration scenarios (DAC-019..DAC-026) one-to-one with the shipped MCP surface — idempotent upsert + self-loop rejection, empty-input no-op, hostile-metadata redaction, five query modes, three convergence buckets, recovery payload, derived-projection replay contract, tool-family separation. Phase 005 added 6 value-comparison scenarios (DAC-027..DAC-032) that contrast the no-graph operator baseline workflow against the with-graph workflow on real-world situations: unresolved-disagreement triage, decision provenance audit, convergence safety under critical disagreement (the graph blocks a stop the naive two-of-three baseline would allow), stalled-council blocker ranking, hot-topic discovery, mid-run interruption recovery. Phase 005 also repaired two pre-existing vitest failures (Claude translated frontmatter parity assertion, stale persist-artifacts HELPER_PATH).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:29:The council vitest matrix is now one command: `npm run --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server test:council` runs nine vitest files in ~2 seconds. The standalone bash runner at `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/test-council-matrix.sh` wraps that matrix with sk-doc validation plus strict parent spec validation. A 32-entry feature catalog under `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/` mirrors the playbook 1:1, replacing the previous "No feature catalog exists yet" placeholders. A reverse-anchor meta-test at `mcp_server/tests/council-playbook-anchor-integrity.vitest.ts` reads the playbook and asserts every referenced vitest file and test-name string resolves to a real test — so renaming a test name surfaces a playbook update need immediately. The DAC-025 replay path is now executable via `scripts/replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs` — a pure-Node CJS helper that reads `ai-council-state.jsonl` and emits a `council_graph_upsert` payload. The Codex TOML runtime mirror's name and description prose now have parity assertions alongside the existing sandbox/permissions checks. Inline normalization comments in `tests/fixtures/council-value/seed-helpers.ts` document why DAC-030 and DAC-032 fixture-side answers are derived from grouped runtime responses.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:35:This final phase surfaces the new artifacts through the skill entry point. SKILL.md now references `feature_catalog/` and `scripts/replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs` in the Resource Domains section. The smoke vitest at `tests/council-helpers-smoke.vitest.ts` covers the replay helper end-to-end on a synthetic JSONL fixture plus a shape assertion on the bash runner. The `test:council` npm script grew to ten vitest files.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:43:The new `lib/council-graph/` package owns SQLite-backed derived storage. `council-graph-db.ts` exposes idempotent upsert with strict-schema validation (rejects self-loop edges, treats empty input as explicit no-op success, redacts arbitrary metadata to an allowlist of scalar fields before serializing). `council-graph-query.ts` exposes the five query modes with bounded outputs and prompt-safe metadata. `handlers/council-graph/` exposes four MCP entrypoints — upsert, query, status, convergence — that wrap the lib with input validation and prompt-safe response shaping. The graph is a derived projection by design: rows can be deleted scoped to `(specFolder, sessionId)` and replayed from `ai-council/**` artifacts without touching the artifacts themselves.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:47:The value-scenario harness uses shared seed helpers (`seed-helpers.ts`) plus per-scenario fixtures (`dac-027.ts` through `dac-032.ts`) plus shared seed-data JSON consumed by both vitest and the operator seeder (`scripts/seed-council-value-fixture.cjs`). Each test opens a fresh in-memory SQLite DB, seeds the graph through the same upsert handler the runtime uses, runs both the no-graph baseline (`fs.readdirSync` + grep predicates) and the with-graph workflow (MCP handler calls), then asserts answer equivalence and writes measured metrics to `council-graph-value-report.json`. Temp directories clean up between tests for isolation.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:51:The council test gate is local-only by design — no GitHub Actions. Two invocation paths share one matrix definition: `npm run test:council` (fastest, vitest only) and `bash test-council-matrix.sh` (vitest + sk-doc + strict spec validate, ~5 seconds).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:60:| Measured baseline-vs-graph ratios (Phase 006)          | PASS — 1×-13× per scenario, persisted to JSON       |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:73:- `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs --spec-folder <path> --session-id <id> [--dry-run]` — read `ai-council-state.jsonl` and emit a `council_graph_upsert` payload for operator-piped MCP replay.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:74:- `bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/test-council-matrix.sh` — run the full council vitest matrix plus sk-doc + strict spec validation in one command.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:82:No migration is required. Phase 003's council graph is a derived projection — existing `ai-council/` artifacts produced before this release remain authoritative and replayable. Callers already using `@deep-ai-council` need no changes; the agent body and runtime mirrors are stable. The playbook count grew from 18 to 32 scenarios; if external tooling counts playbook entries, update those counters.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:1:# v1.3.0.0 — Skill rename sk-ai-council → deep-ai-council (v1.x single-round council era)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:7:- Renamed the skill folder `.opencode/skills/sk-ai-council/` to `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:9:- Bumped `SKILL.md` frontmatter to `name: deep-ai-council` and `version: 1.3.0.0`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:10:- Kept the runtime agent slug at `ai-council`; this packet is skill-folder-only.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.3.0.0.md:14:The council skill is rejoining the `deep-*` family alongside `deep-review`, `deep-research`, `deep-agent-improvement`, and `deep-loop-runtime`, following the 130 research verdict and user intent. This is a point-release in the v1.x single-round-council era because it renames the skill package without changing the runtime model. The runtime agent remains `ai-council` because agent identity is already decoupled from the skill folder slug.

The playbook scenario `08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-query-hostile-metadata-redaction.md` defines the operator prompt, command sequence, expected signals, evidence, and pass/fail criteria for DAC-021.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-query-hostile-metadata-redaction.md:45:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md` | Reference | Documents the allowlist contract |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-query-hostile-metadata-redaction.md:3:description: "This scenario validates that `runtime query CLI` redacts arbitrary metadata keys and bounds string lengths before returning prompt-safe output. Anchors to council-graph-script.vitest.ts test 'redacts arbitrary metadata from prompt-safe query output'."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-query-hostile-metadata-redaction.md:15:This scenario validates that `runtime query CLI` returns prompt-safe metadata only — even when the underlying graph row contains arbitrary keys, oversized strings, or hostile payloads injected during reducer/upsert. The query handler must redact non-allowlisted keys and truncate long strings before serializing into the response.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-query-hostile-metadata-redaction.md:29:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, upsert a council graph node with hostile metadata (arbitrary keys, oversized strings) and then call runtime query CLI; assert the response strips arbitrary keys and bounds string lengths.`

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/loop_contract.md:78:Every per-sample grade is journaled to `benchmark/_loop/state/loop-journal.jsonl` as an append-only JSONL file. Events include:
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/loop_contract.md:95:- **`worktree_kept`** — accepted promotion worktree
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/loop_contract.md:108:- **`promotedStop`** (canonical: `converged`) — First accepted promotion ends the session; the kept worktree awaits operator merge, and re-sampling unchanged HEAD could not observe the improvement
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/loop_contract.md:135:A session ends at the FIRST accepted promotion (`promotedStop`): later iterations would
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/loop_contract.md:139:should assume a full `--max-iters` run only when no candidate is accepted and no hard
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/loop_contract.md:166:`scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs` resolves `--mode=non-dev-ai-system-refine`, validates against `VALID_MODES`, and plans a single adapter step pointing at `run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs`. The adapter:
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:3:description: "Real-world scenario where the graph PREVENTS premature stop. Two-of-three rule (no-graph baseline) would trigger convergence; graph returns STOP_BLOCKED because one critical disagreement is unresolved. Anchors to `runtime convergence CLI`."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:15:This scenario validates that `runtime convergence CLI` provides a safety guarantee the no-graph two-of-three rule cannot: it blocks `STOP` decisions when a critical disagreement remains unresolved, even if 2 of 3 seats otherwise agree.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:19:The two-of-three convergence rule is documented in `references/convergence/convergence_signals.md` and is the no-graph baseline for stop decisions. But "2 of 3 agree" can mask unresolved critical issues: seats A and B may both endorse Plan X while seat C's unresolved security concern about Plan X gets lost. Without graph: convergence triggers and the issue ships. With graph: `unresolvedCriticalDisagreements` is a hard `STOP_BLOCKED` signal — graph prevents premature convergence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:29:- Objective: Demonstrate the graph provides a safety guarantee (block on unresolved critical) that the two-of-three baseline does not.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:32:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, evaluate convergence on a seeded session where 2 of 3 seats agree on Plan X but seat C raised an unresolved critical disagreement; confirm the graph returns STOP_BLOCKED with the critical disagreement cited, contradicting the naive two-of-three baseline.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:34:- Expected signals: Baseline conclusion: 2 of 3 → stop allowed. Graph conclusion: `STOP_BLOCKED` with reason trace citing `d1` and `unresolvedCriticalDisagreements ≥ 1`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:35:- Desired user-visible outcome: Operator sees the graph protected against shipping an unresolved critical that the baseline would have missed.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:36:- Pass/fail: PASS if graph returns `STOP_BLOCKED` and baseline would allow stop; FAIL if graph returns `STOP_ALLOWED` or `CONTINUE` despite the unresolved critical.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:51:`As a council-graph integration validator, evaluate convergence on a seeded session where 2 of 3 seats agree on Plan X but seat C raised an unresolved critical disagreement; confirm the graph returns STOP_BLOCKED with the critical disagreement cited, contradicting the naive two-of-three baseline.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:67:Baseline conclusion: 2 of 3 agreement → stop allowed (per documented rule). Graph conclusion: `decision: 'STOP_BLOCKED'`, reason trace cites `d1` and `unresolvedCriticalDisagreements >= 1`. The two answers disagree, and the graph's answer is the safety-preserving one.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:76:- **Fail**: Graph returns `STOP_ALLOWED` or `CONTINUE` despite the unresolved critical. Reason trace omits `d1` or the critical-severity signal.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/convergence-safety-under-critical-disagreement-graph-vs-baseline.md:80:If graph returns `STOP_ALLOWED`, inspect `scripts/convergence.cjs` for the `unresolvedCriticalDisagreements` short-circuit (P1-002 remediation should make any non-zero critical force `STOP_BLOCKED`). If `CONTINUE` returned, the critical-severity classification on `d1` may not be flowing through `getUnresolvedCriticalDisagreements`.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/convergence_signals.md:40:ADR-001 keeps the convention lightweight. Sophisticated convergence math is non-goal N1 because the packet needs auditable output persistence, not a deep-skill state machine.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/convergence_signals.md:46:State schema and convergence fields are convention-only for v1. If drift appears in real council artifacts, graduate to a typed validator in a follow-on packet.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/convergence_signals.md:49:- Agent body: `.opencode/agents/ai-council.md` §16
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/convergence_signals.md:58:**Semantic:** the deep-ai-council threshold scores per-topic Round-N -> Round-N+1 verdict deltas from the adjudicator. Lower = more rounds / higher stability threshold.

It focuses on rubric weights and comparison-table application."

It focuses on matching agent §6 weights and applying them in a comparison table row.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/operator_guide.md:80:3. **Held-out promotion gate.** Candidates are accepted only on non-regression of the independent grade on held-out fixtures the proposer never sees, measured against a pre-edit baseline in the same worktree. The baseline is measured once per session and reused for later iterations (HEAD cannot change mid-run, so re-measuring it would burn dispatches for no new signal); candidate measurements are always fresh. Held-out fixtures must produce gradeable deliverables (interactive fixtures that answer with a question cannot be graded).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/operator_guide.md:82:5. **Worktree isolation, always cleaned.** Edits happen in a worktree detached at HEAD (`loop.py --run` refuses a dirty source tree, including uncommitted edits to symlinked shared-global docs). Accepted candidates stay in their kept worktree (detached at the candidate state) for deliberate operator merge; rejected ones are removed, including on kill-switch exits. The first accepted promotion ends the session (`promotedStop`, canonical `converged`, with the kept-worktree path printed and journaled) — further iterations would re-sample unchanged HEAD and could never observe the kept improvement, so at most one worktree is kept per run.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/operator_guide.md:97:Template provenance and versioning: the templates parameterize the pilot implementations, including every deep-review guardrail fix, and carry a kit version (`kit_version` in the config, current 1.3.0; 1.2.0 added derived-copy `mode: symlink` with `link_target` and the `transform: skill_strip` sk-doc alignment for skill copies; 1.3.0 added per-entry `src_root` overrides on `derived_copies` (asset mirrors, SKILL.md byte-mirrors) and the gauntlet's live-packaging overlay — the attack worktree now mirrors the live tree instead of git HEAD, so uncommitted layout changes are testable, with attack resets restoring from the live tree). A packaging owns its rendered instance afterwards. The maintenance model is TEMPLATE-AS-SOURCE for new packagings and instance-owned thereafter: live instances may evolve ahead of the kit (the pilots do), and that drift is accepted, not a defect. When the kit advances: bump `kit_version` in your config, run `init_packaging.py --check-only` against it, and diff the temp render against your live instance to review what the new kit would change before adopting any of it. Fixes that harden guardrails should land in BOTH the kit and the live instances in the same change.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/README.md:3:description: "Lane C scripts that benchmark a skill's real-world routing, discovery, efficiency, and usefulness — parse its playbook into gold, dispatch per scenario, score D1-D5, and render a dual report."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/README.md:17:`skill-benchmark/` owns the Lane C (skill-benchmark) executables: benchmark a target skill's live routing, discovery, efficiency, and usefulness against its own authored `manual_testing_playbook`, then score the result on five dimensions and render a dual machine/human report. Private gold never crosses the dispatch boundary — scenarios are dispatched on a public prompt and joined back to gold only at scoring time.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/README.md:51:│ executor-        │ ─routing/  │ router-replay.cjs   (Mode A)     │
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/README.md:105:| `executor-dispatch.cjs` | The seam between orchestrator and executors. Routes `classKind` routing/advisor + trace-mode router→`router-replay`, +live→`live-executor`, browser→`browser-executor`, and normalizes each into one observed-result the scorer consumes. Lazy-requires live/browser siblings. Exports `dispatchScenario`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/README.md:107:| `live-executor.cjs` | Mode B live executor via `opencode run`. Runs routing/advisor scenarios as routing-ANALYSIS prompts, reads back stated routing + tool_use corroboration, normalizes to the observed-result shape. Self-contained dispatch (no `--agent`, uses `--format json`). Exports `runLiveScenario`, `parseLiveResult`, `buildLiveDispatchPrompt`, `runDispatch`, and parsing helpers. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/README.md:114:| `playbook-generator.cjs` | Opt-in staged scenario generator for skills with thin coverage. NEVER writes the live playbook — writes only to `manual_testing_playbook/_generated_staging/` and emits a promoteHint. Generated scenarios are tier `T1-auto` and must pass contamination, parser round-trip, structural, and self-consistency gates. Exports `generatePlaybook`, `analyzeCoverage`, `validateGenerated`, `renderScenarioMarkdown`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/README.md:126:| Ownership | Lane C dispatch, scoring, and reporting live here. The 5-dimension grader and grader prompts are reused from `../model-benchmark/scorer/`. Mode routing and lane-path resolution live in `../shared/loop-host.cjs`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/README.md:127:| Write policy | Diagnostic-by-default. The only writers are `build-report.cjs` (`report.md`, from JSON) and the orchestrator (`report.json`, plus `--d4` augmentation). `playbook-generator.cjs` writes only to a skill's `manual_testing_playbook/_generated_staging/`, opt-in via `createMissing`. Live dispatch requires `--trace-mode live`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/executor-dispatch.cjs:13: *   classKind routing/advisor + trace-mode router → routeSkillResources (deterministic)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/executor-dispatch.cjs:14: *   classKind routing/advisor + trace-mode live   → live-executor.cjs (cli-opencode)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/executor-dispatch.cjs:55:  const classKind = scenario.classKind || 'routing';
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/executor-dispatch.cjs:59:    // in router (CI) mode keep them routed-out so the gate stays deterministic
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:2:title: "DAC-027 -- Unresolved disagreement triage: graph vs no-graph baseline"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:3:description: "Real-world scenario comparing operator effort to identify unresolved critical disagreements after a multi-round council, with vs without the council graph. Anchors to `runtime query CLI mode='unresolved_disagreements'`."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:7:# DAC-027 -- Unresolved disagreement triage: graph vs no-graph baseline
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:15:This scenario validates that `runtime query CLI mode='unresolved_disagreements'` materially beats the no-graph baseline for the common operator task: "after a 3-round council, which critical disagreements were never resolved?"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:19:Council deliberation produces 5+ artifacts per seat per round. A 3-round, 3-seat run yields ≥45 artifact files. Triaging which DISAGREEMENT nodes remain unresolved by re-reading every critique is slow, error-prone, and breaks down when reviewing weeks-old sessions. The graph collapses this to one runtime CLI call.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:29:- Objective: Demonstrate measurable effort reduction between no-graph baseline and graph-driven workflow for finding unresolved critical disagreements.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:30:- Real-world situation: A 3-round, 3-seat council shipped 4 DISAGREEMENT nodes. Seat A and Seat B reached agreement on 2 of them via RESOLVES edges in round 3. The remaining 2 carry `severity: 'critical'` and have no RESOLVES edge. Operator needs to list the unresolved criticals before declaring convergence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:31:- Real user request: After this 3-round council finishes, give me the critical disagreements that never got resolved.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:32:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, run the unresolved-disagreement triage on a seeded 3-round council session — first via the no-graph baseline (operator reads artifacts), then via runtime query CLI with mode unresolved_disagreements — and confirm the with-graph path returns the same 2 critical unresolved nodes in one runtime CLI call.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:33:- Expected execution process: Seed graph with 3 rounds, 3 seats, 12 claims, 4 disagreements (2 resolved via RESOLVES edges in round 3, 2 critical unresolved). Run baseline workflow and graph workflow. Compare answers and effort.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:34:- Expected signals: Both workflows identify the same 2 unresolved critical disagreement nodes. Graph workflow uses 1 runtime CLI call returning bounded prompt-safe metadata. Baseline workflow requires reading ≥12 artifact files manually.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:36:- Pass/fail: PASS if both workflows agree on the unresolved set AND the graph workflow uses materially fewer reads; FAIL if answers diverge or the graph response includes resolved disagreements.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:45:2. Run the **no-graph baseline workflow**: list every file under `ai-council/deliberations/**` and `ai-council/critiques/**`, read each, extract DISAGREEMENT mentions, cross-reference resolution language, build the unresolved list. Capture file-read count and conclusion.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:46:3. Run the **with-graph workflow**: one runtime CLI call `runtime query CLI mode='unresolved_disagreements' limit:10`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:51:`As a council-graph integration validator, run the unresolved-disagreement triage on a seeded 3-round council session — first via the no-graph baseline (operator reads artifacts), then via runtime query CLI with mode unresolved_disagreements — and confirm the with-graph path returns the same 2 critical unresolved nodes in one runtime CLI call.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:58:2. `bash: for f in <spec-folder>*.md <spec-folder>*.md; do rg -l 'DISAGREEMENT|disagreement|conflict|unresolved' "$f" 2>/dev/null; done` (manual review of each hit follows)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:64:5. `tool: runtime query CLI({ specFolder: 'sandbox/dac-027', sessionId: 'dac-027-run-01', mode: 'unresolved_disagreements', limit: 10 })`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:68:Both workflows identify the same 2 unresolved critical disagreement nodes (`d1`, `d3`). Graph workflow uses 1 runtime CLI call with bounded prompt-safe response. Baseline workflow requires reading 12+ artifact files and cross-referencing resolution language manually.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:72:Capture: baseline file-read count, baseline conclusion, graph response JSON, and a one-line value delta ("graph cut effort from 12 file reads to 1 runtime CLI call").
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:76:- **Pass**: Both workflows agree on the unresolved set. Graph response contains only `d1` and `d3`. Baseline requires materially more effort (≥10× the file reads).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:77:- **Fail**: Answers diverge. Graph response includes resolved nodes `d2` or `d4`. Graph response missing `d1` or `d3`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/09--council-graph-value-comparison/unresolved-disagreement-triage-graph-vs-baseline.md:81:If graph response includes resolved disagreements, inspect `lib/council/council-graph-query.ts` `getUnresolvedDisagreements` for RESOLVES-edge filtering regression. If answers diverge between workflows, the seed data does not match the documented contract — re-seed and retry.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:5:  - "deep-ai-council scoring rubric"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:37:The scoring output belongs in the Multi-AI Council Report comparison table and in any persisted deliberation artifact. The score is not a popularity vote. It is an auditable planning judgment based on evidence, risk, completeness, and fit.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:45:Use the score to clarify trade-offs. Do not use it to hide unresolved contradictions.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:55:| Correctness | 30% | Solves the stated problem completely | 30=perfect, 20=mostly, 10=partial, 0=wrong |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:147:- the leading plan has unresolved high-severity risk;
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:155:- present unresolved alternatives with trade-offs;
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:170:Skip only when one seat leads by 25+ points and no critical risk is unresolved.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:216:- For a defended trade-off, keep the score stable.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:246:- keep simulated-vantage labels visible.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:265:| Strategy timeout/failure | Score remaining seats, note incomplete data |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:280:Attribute the recommended plan, validation steps, risk mitigations, dropped alternatives, unresolved trade-offs, assumptions, and confidence rationale.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md:340:- Agent body: `.opencode/agents/ai-council.md` §6 SYNTHESIS PROTOCOL.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:5:  - "deep-ai-council graph support"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:16:Dedicated council graph support is available as a derived `deep-loop-runtime` CLI projection. It does not replace packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts or append-only state.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:28:- `ai-council/deliberations/**`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:29:- `ai-council/critiques/**`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:30:- `ai-council/council-report.md`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:41:- `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/query.cjs --loop-type council` reads unresolved disagreements, evidence chains, decision support, convergence blockers, and hot nodes.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:88:- `unresolvedCriticalDisagreements`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:91:An empty graph returns `STOP_BLOCKED`, not false-safe success. Critical unresolved disagreements also block stop approval.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:101:1. Keep `ai-council/**` artifacts unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:103:3. Replay derived nodes and edges from packet-local artifacts with `scripts/replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs`, which reads `ai-council-state.jsonl` and calls the runtime `upsert.cjs --loop-type council` path across the SESSION / ROUND / SEAT / CLAIM / EVIDENCE / DISAGREEMENT / DECISION / RECOMMENDATION node kinds. Use `--dry-run` to inspect the derived payload without writing.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:108:Never rewrite historical `ai-council-state.jsonl` rows to fit the graph.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/integration/graph_support.md:118:- Graph replay script: `scripts/replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs` (derives the council payload from `ai-council-state.jsonl` and writes it through `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --loop-type council`; run it after deleting stale derived rows during recovery)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-convergence-three-state-decision-matrix.md:3:description: "This scenario validates that `runtime convergence CLI` emits `CONTINUE`, `STOP_ALLOWED`, or `STOP_BLOCKED` based on agreement, evidence, confidence, and unresolved-critical-disagreement signals for `DAC-023`. Anchors to council-graph-script.vitest.ts tests for all three convergence buckets."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-convergence-three-state-decision-matrix.md:15:This scenario validates that `runtime convergence CLI` correctly classifies a session into one of three decision buckets — `STOP_ALLOWED`, `CONTINUE`, or `STOP_BLOCKED` — based on the documented signals (`agreementRatio`, `dissentDensity`, `evidenceDepth`, `unresolvedCriticalDisagreements`, `decisionConfidence`).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-convergence-three-state-decision-matrix.md:29:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, seed three council-graph sessions hitting STOP_ALLOWED, CONTINUE, and STOP_BLOCKED branches; call runtime convergence CLI on each and assert the returned decision bucket and reason trace.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-convergence-three-state-decision-matrix.md:30:- Expected execution process: Build three sessions: (a) high agreement + sufficient evidence + no critical disagreement; (b) below-threshold agreement + decent evidence + no critical disagreement; (c) critical unresolved disagreement regardless of agreement; call `runtime convergence CLI` on each.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-convergence-three-state-decision-matrix.md:49:`As a council-graph integration validator, seed three council-graph sessions hitting STOP_ALLOWED, CONTINUE, and STOP_BLOCKED branches; call runtime convergence CLI on each and assert the returned decision bucket and reason trace.`

If critical disagreement does not block, inspect `lib/council/council-graph-query.ts` `unresolvedCriticalDisagreements` calc + threshold logic. The migration keeps that semantic boundary but moves ownership out of mk-spec-memory: council state is now a runtime-owned derived SQLite projection, rebuilt from `ai-council/**` artifacts by the replay helper or direct runtime CLI calls.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/non_dev_ai_system/guardrails_teachings.md:153:The promotion gate must be validated end-to-end before trusting it on real improvements. .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/failure_handling.md:5:  - "deep-ai-council failure handling"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/failure_handling.md:25:A failed seat, timeout, unresolved contradiction, or missing external vantage is not an excuse to invent a confident plan. It is evidence that the synthesis must continue with reduced confidence, escalate to the user, or roll back an unsalvageable round.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/failure_handling.md:80:- keep the sequential thinking trace honest;
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/failure_handling.md:83:Depth 1 does not get a free pass to pretend every lens completed. It should still record missing coverage.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/failure_handling.md:135:All-seat failure should not produce a normal `council_complete` with convergence `true`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/failure_handling.md:206:Failures belong in `ai-council-state.jsonl`.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/depth_dispatch.md:5:  - "deep-ai-council depth dispatch"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/depth_dispatch.md:21:Deep mode is the iterative, multi-topic council workflow exposed through `/deep:ai-council`. It is additive to the existing single-round council behavior: regular `ai-council` runs still produce one planning report and packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts, while deep mode owns a session loop with topic-by-topic rounds, adjudicator-verdict stability checks, and a session-wide findings registry.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/depth_dispatch.md:23:Use `/deep:ai-council:auto` for non-interactive bounded runs when setup answers are pre-bound, and `/deep:ai-council:confirm` when the operator should approve setup, loop, synthesis, and save gates. The command Markdown owns setup resolution and then loads `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_auto.yaml` or `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_confirm.yaml` for execution.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/depth_dispatch.md:60:- an orchestrator dispatches `@deep-ai-council`;
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/depth_dispatch.md:191:4. Keep a running comparison as each seat completes.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/depth_dispatch.md:250:Do not invoke `@deep-ai-council` while already inside a council run.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/convergence/depth_dispatch.md:265:Run another council to validate this...
