.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:3:description: "This scenario validates that deleting `council-graph.sqlite` rows for a session and replaying upserts from `ai-council/**` artifacts restores graph state without touching the artifacts. Anchors to ADR-001 derived-projection contract and checklist CHK-028."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:15:This scenario validates the derived-projection contract: council graph rows can be deleted (scoped to one `(specFolder, sessionId)`) and rebuilt by replaying upserts from packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts. The artifacts remain authoritative throughout — they are never read-modified during recovery.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:19:ADR-001 explicitly chose a derived projection over deep-loop graph reuse precisely so council audit history (in `ai-council-state.jsonl` and packet artifacts) stays append-only and trustworthy. Without an exercised replay path, the derived contract is asserted by docs only. This scenario proves the path works end-to-end.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:29:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, capture pre-deletion graph counts, delete derived rows for one (specFolder, sessionId), replay upserts from ai-council/** artifacts, then assert post-rebuild counts match and artifacts were never modified.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:30:- Expected execution process: Snapshot `runtime status CLI` counts + `ai-council/**` artifact mtimes for one session; delete the session's derived rows (via SQL or scripted helper); replay upserts from the artifact tree; re-run status; compare.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:31:- Expected signals: Post-rebuild counts equal pre-deletion counts; `ai-council/**` artifact mtimes unchanged; no error during replay.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:41:1. Pick a healthy session with persisted artifacts (e.g., `specFolder='sandbox/dac-025', sessionId='dac-025-run-01'`); confirm `ai-council/ai-council-state.jsonl` exists with rows.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:45:5. Replay upserts with `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs --spec-folder <spec-folder> --session-id dac-025-run-01`, then pipe the emitted payload to `runtime upsert CLI`.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-derived-projection-rebuilds-from-artifacts.md:78:If counts diverge, inspect the replay loop for missing event types or out-of-order processing. If artifact mtimes change, the replay path or upsert handler is incorrectly writing back into `ai-council/**` — inspect `scripts/upsert.cjs` and the replay helper for write boundaries.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:5:  - "deep-ai-council command wiring"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:16:This reference defines the canonical post-dispatch wiring for callers that run `@deep-ai-council` and need durable packet artifacts.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:22:`@deep-ai-council` is planning-only. It returns a council report and cannot write, edit, patch, or run shell commands. The dispatching parent owns persistence by invoking `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs` after the report is captured.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:24:Future `/speckit:*` consumers and CLI-skill playbooks should use the same helper invocation so council artifacts, state JSONL, and optional memory-save payloads stay consistent.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:32:A user or top-level assistant dispatches `@deep-ai-council`, stores the returned report as `council-report.md`, then invokes the helper.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:38:`@orchestrate` dispatches `@deep-ai-council` as a LEAF. The LEAF returns report text only. `@orchestrate` then persists artifacts using the helper from its own write-capable context.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:42:### `/speckit:*` YAML Workflow
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:44:A command YAML dispatches the council as a planning step and follows it with a persistence step. The command workflow owns the helper invocation, not the council agent.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:64:# 2. Persist packet-local ai-council artifacts and emit optional memory payload.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:65:node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs "$PACKET" \
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:74:The payload uses existing `generate-context.js` categories such as decision record, implementation summary, and handover routing. It does not create a new ANCHOR family.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/shared/promotion_rules.md:6:  - "keep vs reject candidate"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/MODES.md:7:The `mode` string is validated against a fixed enum in `lib/profile-validator.cjs` (`KNOWN_MODES`). The six valid values are exactly the six below. An unknown mode is rejected before any dispatch.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/MODES.md:166:**Question:** where is a single model weak, even when the easy fixtures are saturated? Sweep the **full tiered fixture taxonomy** against one model and read the per-category × per-tier × per-dimension breakdown. This is the anti-saturation payoff: a frontier model can ace every T1 smoke fixture and still show a clear weakness on a T4 adversarial fixture, and the gate keeps a saturated easy fixture from ever crowning a "winner."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/MODES.md:198:- **Validation first.** `lib/profile-validator.cjs` rejects an unknown `mode`, an unknown `models[].executor`, dimension weights that do not sum to `1.0`, a `correctnessGate.threshold` outside `[0,1]`, and a non-positive `samplesPerCell` — before any dispatch. Keep the example shapes above and the validator stays green.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/README.md:94:| Ownership | These scripts serve the shared deep-improvement host surface. Lane A scripts live in `../agent-improvement/`, Lane B scripts in `../model-benchmark/`, Lane C scripts in `../skill-benchmark/`, and Lane D adapter scripts in `../non-dev-ai-system/`. The spawn map keeps bare names in the plan and resolves the lane directory at spawn time. |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:7:# deep-ai-council Feature Catalog
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:9:Canonical inventory of what the `deep-ai-council` planning skill does today, grouped by category and linked to per-feature files.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:15:`deep-ai-council` runs planning-only councils with two or three distinct reasoning seats, forces cross-seat critique, checks convergence, and persists packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts. This catalog lists the 32 shipped features across 9 categories. Each row links to its per-feature file, and each per-feature file carries the implementation anchors, the manual-testing scenario, and the source metadata.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:17:The manual testing playbook (`manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md`) is the companion validation surface: every feature here maps to a DAC scenario there. Feature IDs use the `DAC-NNN` prefix.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:2:title: "deep-ai-council"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:14:# deep-ai-council
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:16:> Put multiple reasoning lenses on a plan, let them disagree honestly and converge only when the disagreement is resolved.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:25:| **Invoke with** | "ai council", "multi-seat planning", "compare strategies", or the `@ai-council` agent |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:27:| **Produces** | A recommended plan, an append-only state log, per-seat proposals, critiques, failed-round forensics and a final report under `ai-council/` |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:39:`deep-ai-council` runs a planning-only multi-seat council. Two or three seats, each with a distinct reasoning lens from six available (Analytical, Creative, Critical, Pragmatic, Holistic, Research), propose independently. Then three critique roles (Hunter, Skeptic and Referee) attack the leading plan before anything is declared settled. Convergence follows a two-of-three rule: two seats must agree on the material plan and critique must find no new high-severity blocker. The council never implements. It hands the recommended plan plus the full artifact trail to you or your implementation agent.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:47:For a single planning pass, dispatch the `@ai-council` LEAF agent. It returns one report and one artifact tree to your packet folder.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:50:# Auto-routing through the skill advisor
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:54:@ai-council: Compare the streaming ingest plan against the batch ingest plan for specs/042-pipeline/ and persist the artifacts.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:63:/deep:ai-council:auto
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:66:/deep:ai-council:confirm
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:71:**Step 2: Persist the report.** The `@ai-council` agent is scoped-write and persists the canonical packet-local `ai-council/**` artifact set directly. The CLI helper remains available as a fallback for non-council callers that already have a captured report.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:74:node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs specs/042-pipeline \
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:78:Expected result: a packet-local `ai-council/` tree with `ai-council-state.jsonl`, per-seat outputs under `seats/round-NNN/`, deliberation and critique notes and `council-report.md`. A dispatching parent does not need to invoke the helper for normal `@ai-council` persistence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:83:node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/advise-council-completion.cjs specs/042-pipeline
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:86:Expected result: confirmation that `ai-council-state.jsonl` ends with a `council_complete` event.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:96:Second, deliberate. Each seat proposes independently. Then the adversarial critique pass tries to break the leading proposal before agreement is allowed. If the council hits its round cap without convergence, the run completes as non-converged rather than fabricating consensus. Non-converged is an honest answer: it means the evidence did not settle toward one plan.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:98:Third, persist and hand off. The council produces a report with sections mandated by the output schema and writes the canonical packet-local `ai-council/` artifacts when running as the scoped-write agent. The CLI helper is a fallback for non-council callers with a captured report. Failed rounds move under `failed/` so the forensic trail survives. Implementation passes to an implementation agent or the caller.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:124:The council is planning only. It recommends a plan and persists the reasoning. It never writes application code, never edits spec docs outside `ai-council/` and never commits or deploys. Once it hands off, implementation belongs to `@code`, the caller or your own workflow.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:128:The council shares the `deep-loop-runtime` with the other top-level deep-loop personas. The current roster is context, research, review, ai-council and improvement; each owns a different phase of the planning-to-implementation pipeline and none crosses into another's territory.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:141:| `system-spec-kit` | Owns the packet folder where `ai-council/` artifacts live. Validation, resume and continuity all run through it. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:152:| Per-seat files are absent from a persisted run | The report used a composition-table fallback instead of per-seat headings | Confirm the report uses valid per-seat headings or a composition table the parser accepts. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:154:| `advise-council-completion.cjs` reports no `council_complete` event | The final event was never appended to the state log | Re-open the council context and append the completion event, or re-persist from a completed report. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:155:| Advisor does not route "ai council" prompts | Stale or missing skill graph | Rebuild the skill-advisor graph, then retry the routing query. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:163:A: No. It produces planning artifacts and hands off. Implementation belongs to `@code`, the caller or your own workflow. The council never writes application code, never edits spec docs outside `ai-council/` and never commits.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:175:A: Inside your packet folder under `ai-council/`. The append-only `ai-council-state.jsonl` records every event. Per-seat proposals live under `seats/round-NNN/`. Deliberations and critiques live in their own folders. Failed rounds move under `failed/`. Nothing is deleted, and nothing is rewritten.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:179:A: No. All seats inside one round must use the same CLI executor. Seat diversity within a round comes from different models or reasoning lenses on that CLI, not from mixing providers. If you want a different provider's vantage, add a second dedicated round. This keeps each round's sandbox, tool surface and output schema consistent.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/README.md:183:A: The run completes as `non-converged`. This is an honest answer: the evidence did not settle toward one plan. The failed-round forensics show you what blocked convergence. You can inspect them, adjust lenses or the scope and re-run, or accept the partial result and decide yourself.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/shared/heldout_and_gold_sets.md:3:description: Shared evaluation convention for visible, held-out, and gold fixture tiers that keeps deep-improvement promotion evidence honest across lanes.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/shared/heldout_and_gold_sets.md:17:The pilot behind spec 143 proved two things about benchmark-driven improvement: self-reported scores inflate (~+6/25 measured against independent graders) and proposers overfit any fixture they can read (the pilot's adversarial fixture published its own seeds in-repo). This convention is how every deep-improvement lane keeps its evidence honest. It applies to Lane B profiles, Lane D packagings and any future loop that promotes on benchmark evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/shared/heldout_and_gold_sets.md:42:- Grader replies follow a strict-JSON contract (single-line JSON, no fences); parsers stay tolerant and record explicit `grader_error` values — an auth failure must be diagnosable from the record, not parsed as a zero score (teaching T11).

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/README.md:127:| `node dispatch-model.cjs [--executor=...] [--model=...] [--mock] <prompt-file>` | CLI | Runs the dispatcher and prints a JSON envelope plus raw stdout and stderr. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/README.md:142:Expected result: the dispatcher prints a mock JSON envelope and the vitest suites pass.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/operator_guide.md:16:Lane C benchmarks whether a *skill* is well-routed, discoverable, efficient, and useful **in practice** — distinct from `sk-doc`/`validate.sh` (doc shape) and manual testing playbooks (described behavior). It emits a ranked, remediable Skill Benchmark Report.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/operator_guide.md:22:Run Lane C with `loop-host.cjs --mode=skill-benchmark` (or `/deep:skill-benchmark`). The orchestrator runs the D5 structural hard gate first, then per-scenario contamination-lint → router-replay → score, then writes a dual JSON+Markdown report. Mode A is deterministic and is the CI gate; the D1-inter advisor probe, the live trace (Mode B), and the D4-R task-outcome ablation are all opt-in (live/paid), kept off the CI path.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/operator_guide.md:26:> - `--trace-mode live` — real `cli-opencode` dispatch; routing/advisor scenarios are run as routing-analysis prompts (the model states its routing as JSON, graded vs gold + observed activation); browser scenarios (MR/CB) route to a `bdg` browser executor. Add `--d4` for the opt-in **D4-R task-outcome ablation** — the model is asked to *do* a routine task skill-on vs skill-off (a patch plan + verification, not a routing list), claude-graded into an advisory `D4_task_outcome` (the real usefulness measure, separate from the hallucination-grader D4). A↔B divergence + `assetRecall` are reported alongside.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/operator_guide.md:28:> - **Live model guidance:** `gpt-5.5-fast --variant high` completes (~78s); `xhigh` is too slow and times out. Live is advisory (cost + nondeterminism) — the gated verdict stays driven by router mode + the D5 hard gate. Auto-CREATE generator (`playbook-generator.cjs`) is opt-in + staged.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/operator_guide.md:42:`--advisor-mode=python` enables the **D1-inter** advisor probe — the deterministic in-repo SQLite advisor, scored out-of-band so the answer cannot leak. It is **off by default** (and in CI) to keep the pure-router path fast and dependency-free; enable it to lift a Mode A run from 4-dimension to 5-dimension coverage.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/operator_guide.md:54:`--d4` runs a separate task-outcome ablation (skill-on/off) graded by claude and writes an advisory `D4_task_outcome` to the report; it requires `--trace-mode live` (a no-op otherwise) and spends API per scenario. Skill-off keeps a contamination guard — a leaked skill read drops the pair `unscored` rather than faking a score.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/operator_guide.md:63:4. **Report** — `skill-benchmark-report.json` + `skill-benchmark-report.md` (rendered FROM the JSON, anti-drift).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/operator_guide.md:71:| D3 | efficiency (over-routing proxy) | scored |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.7.0.0.md:35:`changelog/v1.5.0.0.md` was the lone changelog carrying YAML frontmatter. It now starts at its existing version header, matching the summary-first convention used by the other nine changelogs.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.7.0.0.md:47:Three reference files and one playbook scenario still carry arc or packet citations inside labeled provenance sections. This pattern almost certainly repeats across the sibling deep-research, deep-review, and deep-ai-council references, so it is deferred to a single cross-skill evergreen sweep rather than a partial per-skill fix.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.7.0.0.md:53:The full audit ledger lives at `005-deep-improvement/audit-findings.jsonl`, schema-validated against its `schemas/audit-finding.schema.json`. Of nine findings, six resolved and three deferred with rationale, with zero P0 or P1 findings left open.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/07--writer-library-contract/five-dimension-scoring-rubric-application.md:29:The shipped surface is anchored by `deep-ai-council`. The playbook scenario `07--writer-library-contract/five-dimension-scoring-rubric-application.md` defines the operator prompt, command sequence, expected signals, evidence, and pass/fail criteria for DAC-015.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/07--writer-library-contract/five-dimension-scoring-rubric-application.md:31:Current behavior is grounded in `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md`, which the scenario identifies as rubric and comparison-table reference. Validation is anchored by `manual_testing_playbook/07--writer-library-contract/five-dimension-scoring-rubric-application.md`, covering manual scenario contract.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/07--writer-library-contract/five-dimension-scoring-rubric-application.md:43:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/scoring/scoring_rubric.md` | Reference | Rubric and comparison-table reference |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/07--writer-library-contract/five-dimension-scoring-rubric-application.md:44:| `.opencode/agents/ai-council.md` | Runtime Mirror | Authoritative synthesis protocol |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:11:- **Machine-readable router for `sk-code`.** Lane C's Mode A needs an `INTENT_SIGNALS` + `RESOURCE_MAP` router to replay. `sk-code` keeps its router in a referenced doc (`references/smart_routing.md`), not inline in `SKILL.md`, so the benchmark couldn't read it. Added a machine-readable router block (DEFAULT_RESOURCE + 14 intents + a RESOURCE_MAP covering every routable content file) to `smart_routing.md`, with a drift-guard regression test that fails if any mapped path goes missing on disk or any routable doc stops being covered.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:13:- **`DEFAULT_RESOURCE` is now a list.** The replay parser, the route builder, and the D5 connectivity coverage check accept a list of always-loaded resources (string form still supported), so a skill's routing preamble — the docs consulted on every route, not mapped to one intent — is modeled honestly and counts as covered rather than orphaned.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:18:- **The playbook is the corpus.** `load-playbook-scenarios.cjs` parses a skill's `manual_testing_playbook/` into normalized gold scenarios, tagged `routing` / `advisor` / `browser` (sk-code → 24 scenarios). Gold is captured as authored, which surfaces router↔gold drift.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:19:- **One corpus, two trace-modes.** `run-skill-benchmark.cjs` scores the same scenarios in `router` (deterministic, the CI gate — real-gold D1-intra/D2/D3 instead of the previous empty-gold placeholders) and `live` (the operator default). `score-skill-benchmark.cjs` keeps a back-compat adapter for the legacy fixture shape and adds A↔B divergence; `build-report.cjs` adds coverage, per-classKind, routed-out, divergence, and contamination sections.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:20:- **Live executor.** `live-executor.cjs` dispatches a scenario as a routing-analysis prompt through `cli-opencode`, parses the real NDJSON event schema, captures activation + observed reads, and extracts the model's stated routing JSON across fenced / bare / prose shapes (cross-model robust).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:23:- **Auto-CREATE generator (opt-in, staged).** `playbook-generator.cjs` can author scenarios for a skill lacking them — opt-in only, writes to a `_generated_staging/` dir (never the live playbook), behind four validation gates (contamination, parser round-trip, structural, self-consistency).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:35:- **Live surface mismatch fails routing.** A live result whose observed surface disagrees with the scenario's expected surface now caps D1-intra below the pass line and reports a `surface-mismatch` funnel stage, instead of passing on incidental resource overlap. Router mode (no observed surface) is unaffected.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:49:- `bdg` headless smoke passed (dom eval + console JSON + stop); skill-off ablation drops sk-code loads to 0.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:50:- `validate.sh --strict` PASSED (0 errors / 0 warnings) on the 122 phase parent and both new phase children.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/05--scope-boundaries/planning-only-boundary-rejects-implementation-writes.md:31:Current behavior is grounded in `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/SKILL.md`, which the scenario identifies as skill rules and integration points. Validation is anchored by `manual_testing_playbook/05--scope-boundaries/planning-only-boundary-rejects-implementation-writes.md`, covering manual scenario contract.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/feature_catalog/05--scope-boundaries/planning-only-boundary-rejects-implementation-writes.md:33:The user-visible contract is concrete: Verify the council writes only packet-local ai-council/** artifacts. The catalog entry mirrors that contract so reviewers can move from feature inventory to the exact playbook scenario and source files without guessing.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/model_benchmark/evaluator_contract.md:69:The scorer emits JSON with:
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/model_benchmark/evaluator_contract.md:80:- `legacyScore` (always `null`; retained for schema compatibility)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/model_benchmark/evaluator_contract.md:82:The benchmark runner emits JSON with:
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/model_benchmark/evaluator_contract.md:109:| System Fitness | 0.15 | Permission-capability alignment (no tools listed but denied); resource references valid (commands and skills exist); frontmatter completeness (name, mode, permission) |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:32:- **D1-intra** = `0.4 * intentRecall + 0.6 * resourceRecall` vs private `expected.intentKeys` / `expected.resources`. Empty expected = not-applicable (treated as 1.0, non-penalizing). Negative-activation scenarios invert: routing the expected resources is a failure.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:34:- **D3** (Mode A proxy) = `1 - wastedRouted / totalRouted` (over-routing penalty). Live mode replaces with calls/tokens-to-first-expected.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:49:- **`D4_task_outcome` — the real usefulness instrument (D4-R).** Opt-in via `--d4` (live). The model is asked to *do* a routine task (a minimal patch plan + verification commands, not a routing list) skill-on vs skill-off; both answers are graded by the task-outcome rubric (`system-grader-task-outcome.md`: correctness / verification-fit / focus / hallucination-risk) through the Lane B grader harness (claude-graded; distinct cache keys via the `#taskoutcome#on|off` fixture ids). Score = `0.5 + (on − off) / 2` (>0.5 = the skill helped). Stamped `attribution: approximate` (skill-off is approximated by hook-disable + preamble + a contamination guard that drops a leaked pair). Reported as `advisorySignals.D4_task_outcome`, never summed into the verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:50:- **`assetRecall` — deferred-asset support.** `expectedAssets` is scored on its own lane (live: recall vs the model's stated assets; router: deferred). `live-executor` keeps assets on a separate `observedAssets` channel so a stated, useful asset is not counted as D3 over-routing waste.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:52:**Live trace (Mode B)** replaces the D2/D3 router-replay proxies with the model's stated/observed routing (references only — assets are on the `assetRecall` lane). Signals that did not run report `status: unscored-mode-a` — never faked.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:60:Per-scenario `firstFailingStage` follows the implemented order: `activated-inter` (advisor selected the wrong skill when D1-inter is scored), `router-unparseable`, `surface-mismatch`, `routed-intra`, then `discovered`. A scenario with no failing stage is counted as `passed`; orchestrator-added degradation rows can also contribute `unparseable-fixture`, `contaminated-fixture`, or browser routed-out reasons, and `build-report.cjs` displays routed-out rows with their reason instead of a normal failing stage. The headline bottleneck is the non-`passed` stage with the largest first-failure count (attrition). Bottlenecks list D5 findings plus the headline funnel finding, each mapped through `assets/skill_benchmark/remediation_taxonomy.json` to file, locus, one-line fix, and handoff lane.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/tests/README.md:23:- Fixture skills used as routing targets are real in-repo skills (`cli-codex`, `sk-code`, `deep-improvement`); router-less negative cases are built from a throwaway temp `SKILL.md`.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:3:description: "Pure, mostly dependency-free helpers consumed by the sweep runner: per-cell scoring, reviewer verdict scoring, correctness gating, framework rendering, profile validation, grouped reporting, and stats."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:25:- `profile-validator.cjs` validates only the new sweep keys, only when present, so legacy profiles pass untouched.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:36:| `reviewer-scorer.cjs` | Scores reviewer-prompt fixtures. Detects `kind: "reviewer-prompt"`, composes prompts from `prompt_template` plus `input`, dispatches through `dispatch-model.cjs` when no deterministic `reviewer_output` is present, extracts `PASS`/`FAIL`/`BLOCK` pattern-first with `--grader llm` fallback, and emits a Lane B report row with `correctness_pass_rate`, D1-D5 dimensions, per-case details, and `REVIEWER_BENCHMARK` mismatch messages. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:38:| `framework-renderer.cjs` | Data-driven `{{slot}}` prompt renderer over a JSON framework registry. Computes a framework-neutral `output_contract` + `constraints` from the fixture, fills every slot, and throws naming any required slot left empty or any placeholder that survived. Exports `renderFramework`, `loadRegistry`, `getFramework`, `DEFAULT_CONSTRAINTS`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:39:| `profile-validator.cjs` | Additive, dependency-free sweep-key validator. Returns `{ valid, errors }` (collects all issues, never throws). A profile with no `mode` is reported valid and untouched; present keys (`mode`, `models[].executor`, `scoring.scorer`, dimension weights summing to 1.0, `correctnessGate.threshold ∈ [0,1]`, `sampling.samplesPerCell`) are checked against their contract. Exports `validateProfile`, `KNOWN_MODES`, `KNOWN_EXECUTORS`, `KNOWN_SCORERS`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:49:| Imports | `code-task-scorer.cjs`, `correctness-gate.cjs`, `framework-renderer.cjs`, `profile-validator.cjs`, and `sweep-stats.cjs` import only Node builtins. `sweep-reporter.cjs` is the one intra-lib edge: it requires `./sweep-stats.cjs` and `./correctness-gate.cjs`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:51:| Consumers | `../sweep-benchmark.cjs` is the sweep runner that composes these: it requires `framework-renderer`, `profile-validator`, `code-task-scorer`, and `sweep-reporter` directly (the gate and stats arrive transitively through the reporter). |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:52:| Ownership | These primitives own per-cell scoring, gating, rendering, validation, aggregation, and stats. The sweep orchestration, dispatch, and disk layout live in `../sweep-benchmark.cjs`; the legacy 5-dimension engine lives in `../scorer/`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:53:| Write policy | Pure helpers do no I/O except `sweep-reporter.report` (writes `aggregate.json` + `synthesis.md` only when `outDir`/`write` is set) and `framework-renderer.loadRegistry` (reads a registry JSON). The child-process scorer materializes a runner program to a temp dir; it writes no benchmark state. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:64:│ profile-validator.validateProfile            │
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/README.md:65:│ (additive sweep-key checks; legacy passes)   │

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:1:This release lands the deep-improvement portion of the packets 108 plus 110 auto-review uplift program. One reusable teaching from the upstream `dzianisv/opencode-plugins` auto-review package now applies to the mutation-coverage pipeline: a signature-based dedup that prevents the same mutation from being proposed twice across iterations of the same improvement arc. The signature is stored alongside each mutation in `mutation-coverage.json`. The council was explicit in section 10.6 that the authoritative storage IS `mutation-coverage.json`, NOT `agent-improvement-state.jsonl`, so the replay consumer and the coverage reducer share one source of truth. Every mechanism cleared a 5-of-5 unanimous deep-ai-council review by `cli-codex gpt-5.5 xhigh fast` before any code shipped.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:17:The naive port would have stored signatures in `agent-improvement-state.jsonl`. The council's section 10.6 review rejected that path because the JSONL state file is event-sourced and append-only, which means dedup queries would have to scan the entire history on every proposal. The signatures live on each mutation entry in `mutation-coverage.json`, which is the reducer's canonical output and is sized to the actual mutation count rather than the iteration count. The replay consumer and the reducer now read signatures from the same place.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:23:`scripts/reduce-state.cjs` recognizes the new `signature` field on every coverage entry it sees. The reducer is backward-compatible: entries that predate this release have no `signature` field, and the reducer falls through to the legacy equality check rather than dropping them. New coverage files written after this release will always carry signatures because `mutation-coverage.cjs` populates the field before any append.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:41:Packet 110 ran through a 5-seat deep-ai-council deliberation before any source change. The council returned REQUEST-CHANGES on the first pass with HIGH confidence and reshaped scope on the storage decision. The original draft proposed `agent-improvement-state.jsonl` as the signature store; the council's section 10.6 ruled that storage had to live on the mutation entry in `mutation-coverage.json` so the replay consumer and reducer share one source of truth. The skip-reason format and the env-var bypass also surfaced from the council deliberation rather than from the original packet draft. Implementation followed via `cli-opencode + deepseek-v4-pro --pure`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:52:A new manual playbook scenario at `manual_testing_playbook/07--runtime-truth/034-replay-consumer.md` verifies that the replay consumer respects signature dedup. The scenario covers the happy path (signature match skips), the legacy path (missing signature falls through to equality), and the bypass path (`DEEP_AGENT_IMPROVEMENT_SKIP_DEDUP=1` re-enables the proposal).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.6.0.0.md:79:No migration required. Existing `mutation-coverage.json` files that predate this release have no `signature` field on their mutation entries; the reducer recognizes this and falls through to the legacy equality check, so historical coverage data continues to function. New mutation proposals after this release will populate the `signature` field on every entry, and dedup will engage on the next iteration. Set `DEEP_AGENT_IMPROVEMENT_SKIP_DEDUP=1` if you need to bypass the check for a specific run; leave it unset for the safe default. Source commit: b29640496.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:3:> Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/014-d4r-grader-fidelity-remediation` (Level 2, complete, strict-validate PASSED). Review of record: sibling phase `013-skill-benchmark-fidelity-track/review/review-report.md` (28 findings, RESOLVED).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:12:- **Fallback parsing no longer mislabels or accepts malformed payloads.** A new `normalizeParsedPayload` helper backs every parse path (strict, fenced, regex, score-only): it stamps the expected `dimId` when one is missing, normalizes a *mismatched* `dim_id` back to the requested dimension with capped confidence and an annotated rationale (new `*_dim_mismatch` parse statuses), and the score-only last resort uses the threaded `dimId` instead of a hardcoded `'D4'`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:18:- **Robust routing-JSON extraction + model default.** `extractRoutingJson` now uses a string-aware brace-balanced scanner (`collectBraceBalancedObjects`) that handles nested-brace routing payloads the old bare-object regex rejected, and `runDispatch` backfills a missing model to `DEFAULT_MODEL`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:23:- **Config-load failures are visible.** `loadConfig` stays silent on a missing optional file (ENOENT) but warns on a malformed-JSON file that does exist, instead of silently returning `{}`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:28:- **`scoreScenario` decomposed (byte-identical math).** The 153-line function is split into named helpers (`normalizeScenarioInput`, `computeSurfaceMatch`, `scoreD1Intra`, `scoreD2`, `scoreD3`, `scoreAssetRecall`, `firstFailingStage`, `modeAScore`, `buildLiveEvidence`); the D1-intra weights and surface-mismatch cap are named constants; mode-A scoring reads `WEIGHTS`; the negative-scenario `wastedCount` semantics are clarified. No score changes. Duplicated grader-base construction (`d4-ablation`) and event-stream parsing (`sweep-benchmark`) were de-duplicated into shared helpers.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:29:- **Docs match the code.** `SKILL.md` gains the `SKILL_BENCHMARK runtime_assets` router branch and the 6 previously-missing scripts in §11; `README.md` reconciles the references count (14→17), the scripts table, the structure block, and the trigger phrases (6→9); `scoring_contract.md` completes the funnel stages and documents `advisorySignals`; the v1.11.0.0 changelog ties the D4-R scenarios to the `DEFAULT_D4R_SCENARIOS` constant.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:35:All 28 findings fixed, 0 deferred. The fixes were drafted by gpt-5.5-fast (high) in an isolated worktree (RM-8 four-layer isolation), reviewed file-by-file, and integrated to main only after the full suite passed. The most consequential change is the grader fallback path: a D4-R result routed through a parser fallback can no longer be silently stamped `D4` or accepted with a missing `dim_id`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:42:- `validate.sh --strict` PASSED (0/0) on phase `014`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.11.1.0.md:46:No migration. All changes are behavior-preserving: the weighted dimensions, the report schema (`skill-benchmark-report.v1`), and the deterministic CI/router path are unchanged. New grader `parse_status` values (`*_dim_mismatch`) only appear when a grader returns a dimension id different from the one requested.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:11:- **Grouped references/ into scoring/, promotion-gates/, integration/, and workflow/** -- the fifteen references sat in one flat directory. They now live under four topic subfolders. The smart router discovers references recursively, so routing behavior is unchanged while navigation gets clearer.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.1.0.md:13:- **Resume/continuation expected behavior retracted to match reality** -- The skill's runtime-truth section and the `/deep:agent-improvement` command definition both listed `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue` as supported lifecycle modes for evaluating an agent across multiple sessions. The shipped workflow has no inputs for any of them, the reducer does not produce ancestry output, and the journal helper has no boundary event for a multi-session run history. The only thing the runtime actually supports is `new` - every `/deep:agent-improvement` invocation starts a fresh session with generation 1. The documentation now says so explicitly. Operators who want to continue an evaluation should archive the prior `improve/` folder under a timestamped path and re-invoke the command. A future release that ships real multi-session run history will arrive with first-class event emission, reducer ancestry output, and replay coverage; until then, treat every invocation as a standalone evaluation.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.16.0.0.md:14:- `packaging_config.schema.json`: `derived_copies[]` gains an optional `src_root` (packaging-relative; default stays `derive_source_root`). Existing configs stay valid and render byte-identically.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.16.0.0.md:16:- `init_packaging.py` validates `src_root` (non-empty, packaging-relative, no leading `/`, no `..`).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.16.0.0.md:20:- `templates/gauntlet.py.template`: the attack worktree is still created from git HEAD for isolation, then the LIVE packaging is rsync-overlaid (`-aL --delete`) so the battery tests current code against current data; `-L` materializes external symlinks (shared globals) as file content, keeping the sandbox self-contained even when symlink targets were renamed without being committed (proven by the 2026-06-11 workspace folder restructure: HEAD still had the old global-KB folder name and overlaid live symlinks broke inside the worktree until -L). Previously the battery was blind to uncommitted layout changes - the consolidation's relocated frozen scoring file (`meqt_scoring.md` / `deal_scoring.md`) did not exist at HEAD and attack A1 crashed with FileNotFoundError instead of exercising the guardrail.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.4.0.0.md:17:- Advisor scoring tables: skill advisor routing and scoring references target the new skill name.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.4.0.0.md:18:- Command surfaces in 3 runtimes: OpenCode/Copilot, Claude, and Codex command surfaces keep `/deep:agent-improvement` while routing references move to the new skill path.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.4.0.0.md:19:- Agent skill-matrix lines in 3 runtimes: OpenCode/Copilot, Claude, and Codex agent matrices keep the then-current `@improve-agent` identity while pointing at `deep-improvement`; v1.5.0.0 later renames the agent itself.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.13.0.0.md:19:| `assets/model_benchmark/auto.yaml` | `output_path` set to hub path | Matches new routing |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.13.0.0.md:20:| `assets/model_benchmark/confirm.yaml` | `output_path` set to hub path | Matches new routing |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.12.0.0.md:1:This release adds the **capability-discrimination layer** to the model-benchmark framework: three new partial-credit fixture packs (hard computational, harder computational, strict-validation), the capability-comparison profiles, a dispatch cwd-isolation fix that keeps real runs from polluting the repo, and the first **reproducible MiniMax-M3 vs MiMo-V2.5-Pro reliability verdict**. Everything is additive over the existing Lane B grader and the config-driven sweep — the full Vitest suite stays green at **158**.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.12.0.0.md:3:> Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/127-reusable-model-benchmark-framework` — phases `004-capability-discrimination`, `005-sharper-discrimination` (negative result), and `006-strict-validation-fixtures` (Level 2, all strict-validate PASSED). Per-phase verdicts live in each phase's `eval/synthesis.md`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.12.0.0.md:11:- **Hard pack (T4)** — `hard-merge-intervals`, `hard-parse-csv-line`, `hard-roman-to-int`, `hard-eval-expr`: partial-credit oracle fixtures (16–18 cases each), every oracle validated through the real `code-task-scorer.cjs` (reference impl 1.0, wrong impl 0.33–0.625).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.12.0.0.md:13:- **Validation pack (T4)** — `validate-ipv4`, `validate-date`, `validate-semver`: invalid-dominant strict validators (26–28 cases, 64–74% adversarial-invalid; reference 1.0 vs lax 0.75–0.889). These are the **discriminators** — strict input validation, not computation, is where the two models diverge.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.12.0.0.md:18:- `capability-m3-vs-mimo.json` (hard pack, n=3), `capability-m3-vs-mimo-v2.json` (harder pack, n=5), `capability-m3-vs-mimo-v3.json` (validation pack, n=5) — all `mode: model-vs-model`, `correctnessGate.threshold: 1.0`, `reporting.groupBy: model`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.12.0.0.md:31:- First **reproducible** MiniMax-M3 vs MiMo-V2.5-Pro capability verdict: **M3 is the more reliable model** — perfect 1.0 / correctness-gate-eligible across **32 of 32 cells** over two independent runs with disjoint fixtures (004 computational+roman: 1.0 vs 0.898; 006 validation+roman: 1.0 vs 0.891). MiMo carries a **~1-in-5 catastrophic-failure rate** on hard validation plus a mild roman-numeral gap, keeping it below the correctness gate every run.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.14.0.0.md:21:- `scripts/non-dev-ai-system/init_packaging.py`: stdlib-only scaffolder that validates the config, renders `_loop/`, `_gates/` and `benchmark/` into a packaging root and prints the conformance checklist. Motivated by a real hand-port defect that silently disabled floor enforcement for two dimensions.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:17:- **Mode-aware state records:** loop state now carries a `mode` field so a benchmark run reaches `benchmark-complete` and is distinguishable from agent-improvement runs in the journal and registry.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:28:- **Remediated the 3 unique confirmed P1 defects** (phase 004), each with a regression test: the dispatcher dropped the requested working directory for 4 of 5 executors (cwd now propagates to `spawnSync` for all 5), the D3 path guard misclassified sibling-prefix paths (now a separator-bounded prefix guard), acceptance criteria ran un-gated shell commands (now a criteria-exec gate, default ON with a documented opt-out), and the D4 grader accepted unbounded model scores (now clamped to `[0,1]`).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:40:- **New dedicated `/deep:model-benchmark` command** plus two Lane B workflow YAMLs (`deep_model-benchmark_{auto,confirm}.yaml`) that run `loop-host.cjs --mode=model-benchmark`, reduce, journal, and offer mode-aware promotion.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:50:- **`references/` and `assets/` are now organized by lane** (`agent-improvement/`, `model-benchmark/`, `shared/`) rather than by function. This supersedes the v1.8.0.0 function grouping (scoring, promotion-gates, integration, workflow), which was an intermediate step. Every literal was repointed: SKILL.md RESOURCE_MAP, DEFAULT_RESOURCE, RUNTIME_ASSETS, both lanes of command YAMLs, both command docs, README, graph-metadata, scripts, the feature catalog and playbook, and the benchmark profile's internal fixtureDir.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:59:- **F-P2-5 closed:** `reduce-state.cjs` now surfaces the `mode` metadata it already recorded. The registry carries per-profile and global `modes`, and the dashboard prints a "Lane (mode) mix" line in the global summary and each profile section. This is display only, routing is untouched, and a missing mode counts as agent-improvement so legacy ledgers still total. A new regression test covers it.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:85:- Both lanes smoke end to end: agent-improvement scores a candidate, model-benchmark reaches `benchmark-complete` with `pattern` and `5dim`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.9.0.0.md:89:- `validate.sh --strict` on every phase child and `--recursive` on the 121 parent: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:11:The skill writes candidates to packet-local runtime areas instead of editing main agent files directly. Each candidate is scored independently by a deterministic evaluator, benchmarked against target-specific fixture sets, and tracked in an append-only JSONL ledger. Promotion to the main target requires passing all evidence gates plus explicit operator approval.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:31:The `scan-integration.cjs` script discovers every surface an agent touches across the repo: main definition, 3 runtime mirrors (Claude, Codex, .agents), command dispatch references, YAML workflow references, skill mentions, global doc references, and skill advisor routing entries. Mirror sync is detected by signal-string matching against the main body.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:37:The `generate-profile.cjs` script derives a scoring profile from any agent's own structure -- parsing YAML frontmatter, ALWAYS/NEVER/ESCALATE IF rules, output verification checklist, capability scan, anti-patterns, and related resources. This eliminates the need for manually created profiles and makes any agent in `.opencode/agents/` a valid evaluation target.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:51:| System Fitness | 0.15 | Permission-capability alignment, resource references valid |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:101:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/improve_agent-improver_*.yaml` | Created | Auto + confirm YAML workflows |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:1:This release lands Phase 008 of the 042 Deep Research & Review Runtime Improvement Bundle for `deep-improvement`. The skill now (a) actually calls `improvement-journal.cjs` from the visible `/deep:agent-improvement` YAML workflow at every iteration boundary, (b) enforces minimum sample sizes before trade-off and stability verdicts, and (c) implements ADR-002 Option A by reading `improvement-journal.jsonl`, `candidate-run history.json`, and `mutation-coverage.json` during reducer refresh. The visible operator path now emits the richer runtime-truth state the v1.1.0.0 expected behavior promised. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:9:- `SKILL.md` adds a new "Journal Wiring Contract" section documenting the three boundary points, the frozen `STOP_REASONS` / `SESSION_OUTCOMES` enums (aligned to the helper's actual validator: `converged`, `maxIterationsReached`, `blockedStop`, `manualStop`, `error`, `stuckRecovery` / `keptBaseline`, `promoted`, `rolledBack`, `advisoryOnly`), and the orchestrator-only emission rule.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:17:- Corresponding vitest updates: `tests/trade-off-detector.vitest.ts` (17 passing) and `tests/benchmark-stability.vitest.ts` (23 passing) now assert the new insufficient states AND validate configurable overrides.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:23:- **ADR-002 Option A accepted: implement replay consumers** (rather than downgrading docs to ledger-only reality).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:42:- New fixture: `scripts/tests/fixtures/low-sample-benchmark/` - a candidate session with 1 replay + 2-point trade-off trajectory + complete journal/run history/coverage artifacts. Validates that helper smoke tests return `{state: "insufficientSample", replayCount: 1, minRequired: 3}` and `{state: "insufficientData", dataPoints: 2, minRequired: 3}` as expected.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:44:- `032-journal-wiring.md` - verify YAML workflow calls journal helper at all boundaries
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:54:- Journal wiring end-to-end: YAML workflow confirmed to call `improvement-journal.cjs --emit` at session_start + iteration boundary + session_end.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.0.0.md:81:**Backward-compatible.** Existing sessions without the three new replay artifacts (journal, lineage, mutation-coverage) still work - the reducer defaults each consumer field to `null` when the artifact is absent. The `trade-off-detector` and `benchmark-stability` default thresholds (3 data points / 3 replays) are enforced on new sessions but can be overridden via `minDataPoints` / `minReplayCount` constructor options or env variables for legacy replay flows.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.1.0.md:1:Self-test, bug fixes, reducer improvements, and skill advisor routing sync for `deep-improvement`. Phase 009 ran the improvement loop on the agent-improver's own definition and found a real bug (stale command path). Phase 010 fixed that bug plus 4 reducer issues and promoted the best candidate improvements. Phase 011 updated the skill advisor routing and README documentation. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.1.0.md:11:- **Self-test completed** (Phase 009): Ran `/deep:agent-improvement` targeting `agent-improver.md` itself. 3 iterations, baseline 99→100, all 5 dimensions converged at 100. First real end-to-end validation of the skill on its own agent.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.1.0.md:15:- **Accepted counting fix** (Phase 010): Dashboard now counts `candidate-acceptable` and `candidate-better` recommendations as accepted (previously always showed 0).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.1.0.md:16:- **Candidate improvements promoted** (Phase 010): Halt condition for missing inputs, merged checklists, checkbox self-validation, 4th anti-pattern, scan report provenance note, summary box label update.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.0.1.0.md:17:- **Skill advisor routing** (Phase 011): Added 7 INTENT_BOOSTERS, 13 PHRASE_INTENT_BOOSTERS, 8 COMMAND_BRIDGES for 5D scoring, integration scanning, dynamic profiling, and all `/deep:` + `/create:` slash commands.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.2.0.md:1:This patch release focuses on reducer schema mismatches, dead helper code, journal taxonomy gaps, and workflow enforcement. The goal is to make the workflow easier to trust in everyday use.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.2.0.md:32:The deep-improvement loop never emitted `legal_stop_evaluated` or `blocked_stop` events that the other reducers depend on. Both auto and confirm workflow YAMLs now emit structured legal-stop events with gate bundles.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.2.0.md:56:`scan-integration.cjs` received the profile label instead of the real target agent identifier, so it scanned the wrong file. Both workflow YAMLs now pass the real target agent identifier.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.2.2.0.md:89:| Post-fix validation (10 iterations) | PASS - all findings resolved |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:3:description: The onboarding kit gains symlink-mode derived copies and the skill_strip transform, a latent scaffolder bug that rendered JSON booleans into Python source is fixed, and the lane's templates, references and skill docs are aligned with the sk-code and sk-doc standards.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:14:- `packaging_config.schema.json`: `derived_copies[].targets[]` gains three optional fields with behavior-preserving defaults - `mode: copy|symlink`, `link_target` (relative target, required for symlinks, worktree-safe by construction) and `transform: none|skill_strip`. Existing configs stay valid and render byte-identically.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:15:- `templates/derive.py.template`: symlink branches in `derive()` (create relative link) and `check()` (`NOT A SYMLINK` / `BROKEN LINK` / `WRONG TARGET` via basename-of-realpath, worktree-tolerant), plus `_strip_for_skill` (drop TOC section and decorative emojis, keep the do/don't markers) proven regex-equal to the pilot via AST comparison.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:17:- `init_packaging.py` validates the new fields (symlink requires `link_target`, rejects `is_skill_ref`/`transform` combinations); `packaging_config.example.json` mirrors the live pilot (6 symlinked globals, 6 `skill_strip` system docs); operator guide carries the kit 1.2.0 changelog note.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:21:**Problem:** 16 placeholder tokens were rendered with `json.dumps`, so configs containing booleans put JSON `true`/`false` into rendered Python source. Every rendered file compiled (`true` is a valid identifier to the parser) but crashed with `NameError` at import - the latent bug survived all prior `py_compile` smoke checks and surfaced on the first-ever live execution of a rendered `derive.py`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:26:The rendered `derive.py` was executed against the live Copywriter pilot and validated all 24 derived surfaces (12 project mirrors, 6 transformed skill copies, 6 symlinks) - byte parity, not just compile parity. The rendered file was then adopted as the pilot's live `_gates/derive.py`, closing the instance/kit gap for that file. Audit context: an instance-vs-kit review confirmed everything else stays packaging-owned by design (lock, journal, resume, family check), and nothing moves into deep-loop-runtime.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:30:All nine `assets/non_dev_ai_system/templates/` aligned with the sk-code :opencode Python and shell standards: single-line imports, type hints on every function, Google-style docstrings, and WHY-comments on their own line starting with a capital (21 trailing/lowercase comments fixed). Token inventories stayed byte-identical per template, and `run.sh.template` deliberately keeps `set -uo pipefail` without `-e` so the dispatch status report still runs on failure.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:36:- `README.md` rewritten from three to four co-equal lanes (at-a-glance, lanes table, when-to-use, FAQ, related docs); validates with zero issues.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:49:- **Loop efficiency (Fable)**: the held-out baseline is measured once per session and cached (only when measurable), and the first accepted promotion ends the session (`promotedStop`, canonical `converged`) - at most one kept worktree per run, no burned dispatches re-sampling unchanged HEAD.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:51:- **Doc-code mismatches closed**: stale "Packaging-Benchmark-Refine" frontmatter, phantom `run-packaging-refine.cjs` filename, fixture-lint guardrail claims that did not exist, the PR-001 playbook scenario (worktree subdir + mandatory CW_ROOT), the duplicated LOOP_SKIP_PROBE knob, the iteration-keyed grade-record claim, schema editing debris.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:52:- **Scaffolder hardening**: unresolved `{{TOKENS}}` are now fatal at render, derived-copies validation requires src_relpath/relpath and value-checks `transform`, `self_score_regex` is guarded against quote/newline breakage, and the emoji-strip regex no longer eats the Unicode Arrows block (it had corrupted `->` separators in transformed copies).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.15.0.0.md:54:- **Lane D test coverage**: 4 new vitest cases (mode validity, fail-closed, plan shape, ENV_FORWARD map) and a documented battery runner - full battery green (loop-host 25/25; the single failure is the pre-existing Lane C e2e fixture issue).

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:8:- Every playbook scenario, agent mirror, command doc, YAML workflow, asset, and reference path was updated. Legacy `sk-agent-improver` paths are fully removed from live surfaces.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:15:- Frozen `STOP_REASONS` enum published alongside a `SESSION_OUTCOMES` enum so improvement sessions report canonical stop reasons. Current accepted values are documented in `SKILL.md`; deprecated labels such as `convergedImprovement`, `benchmarkPlateau`, `rejectCandidate`, and `rollbackTriggered` are not accepted by the helper validator.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:16:- Added `scripts/improvement-journal.cjs` - append-only JSONL journal with event-type validation. Event types: `session_start`, `candidate_generated`, `scored`, `gate_evaluation`, `session_end`. `session_ended` requires `stopReason` and `sessionOutcome`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:61:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs`                                                       | Created  | Append-only JSONL audit journal with frozen enums and CLI entrypoint                          |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:66:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/tests/improvement-journal.vitest.ts`                                           | Created  | Vitest coverage for journal append/replay and enum validation                                 |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.1.0.0.md:97:> This release documented four lifecycle modes for re-entering an `/deep:agent-improvement` session: `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue`. None of the four were ever wired up. The command has no input for lineage mode, the reducer does not carry ancestry across sessions, and the journal helper has no multi-session boundary event. Every invocation is a fresh `new` session.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.17.0.0.md:8:This release folds the deep-improvement slice of the packet 146 ponytail-based refinement into the agent-mirror surface. Until now, the check that keeps every agent's three runtime copies in agreement only ran inside a deep-improvement promotion. An edit to an agent landed by any other path could silently desync the `.opencode`, `.claude`, and `.codex` mirrors and nothing would catch it until production routing behaved differently per runtime. This release promotes that check to a repo-wide gate, sharpens the normalizer so it stops crying drift over legitimate per-runtime wording, and clears the real and phantom drift the sharper check then surfaced.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.17.0.0.md:26:A gate that runs on every commit has to be precise, and the old normalizer was both too loose and too narrow. Each runtime mirror legitimately describes itself — a clause along the lines of "this runtime's mirror; the canonical source lives in .opencode" — and refers to its own runtime agent-file path (for example `.codex/agents/<name>.toml`), neither of which the canonical file carries. The old `normalizeRuntimeSpecificText` in `scripts/lib/mirror-sync-verify.cjs` counted the self-description clause as body drift and false-flagged healthy mirrors, while a blanket rule that collapsed every bare `.md`/`.toml` extension token was loose enough to mask a real format-extension mismatch. The normalizer now strips the self-description clause and normalizes only the runtime agent-file paths — so a mirror's reference to its own `.codex/agents/<name>.toml` no longer reads as drift — and the blanket bare-extension collapse is gone. The fix is deliberately conservative: it removes only the known self-description and agent-path shapes, and real body drift, including a genuine format-extension mismatch, is still caught.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/changelog/v1.17.0.0.md:32:Running the now-accurate gate over all agents surfaced one genuine divergence and cleared one false positive. The `context` codex mirror had a dropped Tool-Inventory row, which is restored to match the canonical body. The `orchestrate` agent was a false positive that the normalizer fix clears on its own. With both resolved, all twelve agents report in sync.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/SWEEP.md:96:The MVP runs `sweep-benchmark.cjs` standalone and ships the minimum stats needed for an honest verdict. Still on the roadmap: the full stats engine (paired bootstrap confidence intervals / noise-floor gating), the normalized dispatch envelope (OpenCode JSON token/cost parsing and `model-profiles.json` capability fields), the guarded `loop-host.cjs` integration that runs the sweep inside the improvement loop, and the tiered fixture taxonomy (more T3/T4 plus long-context/agentic categories). See the roadmap: `../../../../specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/127-reusable-model-benchmark-framework/001-design-research/research/research.md`.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-grader-task-outcome.md:7:Read the fixture metadata (it carries the task and the pass `rubric`) and the candidate output. Score how useful the output is as a routine-task answer. Return JSON ONLY (no preamble, no markdown, no text outside the JSON object).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-grader-task-outcome.md:27:2. **Verification fit (0.25)** — Does it give a verification command/step that would genuinely confirm THIS change (e.g. the right test runner, type-check, or lint for the language)? Missing or mismatched verification scores low.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-grader-task-outcome.md:40:- Do not score routing recall (which docs were named) — that is D2/D3, handled separately.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-grader-task-outcome.md:43:## Return JSON only
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-grader-task-outcome.md:45:If you find yourself adding commentary, stop and rewrite as the JSON object above. The harness fails-parse and downgrades to a regex fallback otherwise.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/README.md:16:`prompts/` holds the system prompts the grader harness reads at dispatch time. `harness.cjs` loads `system-grader.md` by default and `dispute.cjs` passes `system-skeptic.md` for the adversarial second call. Both prompts instruct the model to score the D4 hallucination dimension and return JSON only.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/README.md:24:| `system-grader.md` | The primary grader. Scores D4 hallucination by checking that every CLI flag, symbol, and path is allowlisted or a known builtin or package. Penalizes invented flags and made-up symbols. Sets confidence below 0.7 when a claim cannot be verified. Requires JSON-only output with `dim_id`, `score`, `confidence`, `rationale`, `evidence`, and `version`. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/README.md:25:| `system-skeptic.md` | The adversarial dispute resolver. Defaults to suspicion and scores high only when every claim is affirmatively verified. Adds an `adversarial_findings` field and is invoked when primary confidence is low or the dispute rate is high. Requires the same JSON-only contract. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-grader.md:7:Read the fixture metadata and the model output. Score the output on the named dimension. Return JSON ONLY (no preamble, no markdown, no explanation outside the JSON object).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-grader.md:43:## Return JSON only
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-grader.md:45:If you find yourself wanting to add commentary, stop and rewrite as JSON. The harness will fail-parse and downgrade to a regex fallback if you don't.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-skeptic.md:7:Read the fixture metadata and the model output. Score the same dimension the primary grader scored, but from an adversarial angle. Return JSON ONLY.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-skeptic.md:39:## Return JSON only
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/scorer/grader/prompts/system-skeptic.md:41:No preamble. No commentary outside the JSON.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/SKILL.md:29:The default and most common council run is a single in-CLI round. Add external rounds only when the active runtime cannot supply the required vantage or when explicit cross-AI validation is requested.
