 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	name: deep-loop-workflows
     3	version: 1.0.0.0
     4	description: "Unified deep-loop workflow skill: routes a request to one of five modes (context, research, review, ai-council, improvement) over the shared deep-loop-runtime backend. Holds no per-mode logic — it dispatches by workflowMode through mode-registry.json. Use for codebase-context gathering, autonomous research, iterative code review, multi-seat AI Council planning, and evaluator-first agent/model/skill/non-dev-system improvement."
     5	allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task, WebFetch]
     6	---
     7	
     8	<!-- Keywords: deep-loop-workflows, deep-loop, deep-context, deep-research, deep-review, deep-ai-council, deep-improvement, context-gathering, reuse-catalog, autoresearch, iterative-research, code-audit, severity-findings, P0-P1-P2, ai-council, council-deliberation, multi-seat-planning, agent-improvement, benchmark-harness, model-benchmark, skill-benchmark, non-dev-ai-system, convergence-detection, externalized-state, coverage-graph, mode-registry, workflowmode, runtimeloop-type, backendkind -->
     9	
    10	# Deep Loop Workflows
    11	
    12	One skill, five workflow modes, one shared backend. `deep-loop-workflows` is the public, advisor-routable home for every deep-loop persona; `deep-loop-runtime` is the frozen, MCP-free backend it consumes. This hub holds NO per-mode convergence, state, or synthesis logic — each mode keeps its own contract in its packet, and the hub only routes by `workflowMode` through `mode-registry.json`.
    13	
    14	---
    15	
    16	## 1. WHEN TO USE
    17	
    18	Use this skill (through the hub) for any deep-loop workflow. Invoke it as `Skill(deep-loop-workflows)` (optionally with a mode hint such as `research: <request>`); the hub classifies the request, resolves a `workflowMode`, and loads the matching nested mode packet. The `/deep:*` commands and native agent types remain as complementary surfaces over the same packets.
    19	
    20	| Mode | Use it for | Packet | Command | Agent |
    21	|------|-----------|--------|---------|-------|
    22	| **context** | Inward codebase-context gathering → reuse-first Context Report, before `/speckit:plan` | `deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/` | `/deep:context` | `deep-context` |
    23	| **research** | Outward, web + code iterative investigation → `research/research.md` | `deep-loop-workflows/deep-research/` | `/deep:research` | `deep-research` |
    24	| **review** | Iterative code audit → P0/P1/P2 findings + verdict | `deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/` | `/deep:review` | `deep-review` |
    25	| **ai-council** | Multi-seat planning deliberation → `ai-council/**` artifacts | `deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/` | `/deep:ai-council` | `ai-council` |
    26	| **improvement** (4 lanes) | Evaluator-first improvement: `agent-improvement`, `model-benchmark`, `skill-benchmark`, `non-dev-ai-system-refine` | `deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/` | `/deep:agent-improvement` · `/deep:model-benchmark` · `/deep:skill-benchmark` · `/deep:ai-system-improvement` | `deep-improvement` |
    27	
    28	### When NOT to Use
    29	- A single quick read/edit (no loop) — use the relevant code or doc skill directly.
    30	- Backend/runtime support (executor, coverage-graph, scoring, fan-out) — that is `deep-loop-runtime`, consumed here, not invoked as a user workflow.
    31	
    32	---
    33	
    34	## 2. SMART ROUTING
    35	
    36	Routing is **registry-driven** (invokable-hub, Option E). `mode-registry.json` is the single source of truth; the hub reads it and does not re-derive the mapping. When invoked as `Skill(deep-loop-workflows[, "<mode>: <request>"])`, the hub classifies the request to a `workflowMode`, resolves it through the registry, and loads `registry[mode].packet`. The advisor routes any deep-loop query to the single identity `deep-loop-workflows`; the hub then picks the mode. The `/deep:*` commands and native agent types remain as complementary surfaces — they reach the same packets through static routers/agent definitions — and the hub holds NO per-mode logic.
    37	
    38	### The three-tier discriminator
    39	- **`workflowMode`** — the public mode key (all modes): `context`, `research`, `review`, `ai-council`, and the four improvement lanes `agent-improvement`, `model-benchmark`, `skill-benchmark`, `ai-system-improvement` (its loop-host mode stays `non-dev-ai-system-refine`).
    40	- **`runtimeLoopType`** — the graph-backed convergence key consumed by `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs` (validated against exactly `research|review|council|context`). **Explicit `null` for all four improvement lanes; never inferred from `workflowMode`.** Note `ai-council` maps to `runtimeLoopType: council`.
    41	- **`backendKind`** — which backend runs the mode: `runtime-loop-type` (context/research/review/ai-council), `improvement-host` (`deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs --mode`), or `external-adapter` (non-dev-ai-system; the loop is owned by external packaging).
    42	
    43	### Routing rule
    44	```
    45	classify the request to a workflowMode (dominant deep-loop intent; mode hint like "research: ..." overrides)
    46	read mode-registry.json
    47	  → resolve workflowMode from the hint / classified intent (or the /deep:* command / advisor alias)
    48	  → load the mode packet at registry[mode].packet/SKILL.md
    49	       e.g. registry["research"].packet → deep-loop-workflows/deep-research/SKILL.md
    50	       (the 4 improvement modes all share the deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/ packet)
    51	  → if registry[mode].runtimeLoopType !== null: backend = convergence.cjs --loop-type <runtimeLoopType>
    52	     else: backend = improvement loop-host (--mode) or external adapter, per backendKind
    53	```
    54	
    55	Intent classification favors the single dominant deep-loop mode; a mode hint (`research: ...`, `review: ...`, `context: ...`, `ai-council: ...`, or an improvement lane) overrides the classifier. The legacy advisor projection maps stay hardcoded and drift-guarded against the registry, and the command files remain static routers with hardcoded asset/mode routing; neither resolves from `mode-registry.json` at runtime, but both stay equal to its projection.
    56	
    57	Per-mode behavior is **not flattened**: each packet keeps its own convergence math, state shape, artifacts, and tool-permission guards (research has WebFetch; review/context/ai-council are code/inward-only; improvement is the only mutating family). Exactly one `graph-metadata.json` — this hub's — is preserved, so the advisor discovers exactly one skill identity regardless of which surface (hub `Skill()`, `/deep:*` command, or agent) reaches a mode.
    58	
    59	---
    60	
    61	## 3. HOW IT WORKS
    62	
    63	### Layout
    64	```
    65	deep-loop-workflows/
    66	  SKILL.md               # this routing hub (no per-mode logic)
    67	  mode-registry.json     # the three-tier discriminator + advisorRouting (single source of truth)
    68	  graph-metadata.json    # the ONE advisor identity for the whole skill
    69	  deep-context/   deep-research/   deep-review/   deep-ai-council/   deep-improvement/   # five verbatim mode packets
    70	  shared/synthesis/      # workflows-shared synthesis (e.g. emitResourceMap)
    71	```
    72	
    73	Each mode packet is the former skill's content moved verbatim (its own `SKILL.md`, `references/`, `scripts/`, `assets/`, `feature_catalog/`, `manual_testing_playbook/`), with internal paths repointed and **no per-packet `graph-metadata.json`** — only this hub carries one, so the advisor discovers exactly one skill. The `deep-ai-council` packet folder follows the standard `folder == packetSkillName` convention (`deep-ai-council`); its legacy public surfaces (the `/deep:ai-council` command and the `ai-council` agent) intentionally keep the shorter `ai-council` key, so always resolve the packet path through `mode-registry.json` rather than hardcoding it.
    74	
    75	### Backend
    76	All modes consume `deep-loop-runtime` (frozen, MCP-free): executor config, prompt-pack, validation, atomic state, coverage-graph, Bayesian scoring, fan-out, the council primitives, and the promoted plumbing (capability resolver, artifact-root, loop-lock CLI, lifecycle taxonomy). The runtime never gains an `improvement` loopType — improvement stays host-driven.
    77	
    78	---
    79	
    80	## 4. RULES
    81	
    82	### ALWAYS
    83	- **ALWAYS** resolve a mode through `mode-registry.json` (read the `packet` key; never hardcode a router mapping or packet path in the hub).
    84	- **ALWAYS** keep advisor projection maps hardcoded and drift-guarded against the registry; command mode routing is still hardcoded in the command files and does not resolve through `mode-registry.json`.
    85	- **ALWAYS** keep each mode's convergence/state/artifact contract in its packet — the hub stays logic-free.
    86	- **ALWAYS** keep exactly one `graph-metadata.json` (this hub's) so the advisor sees one skill identity, whether a mode is reached via `Skill(deep-loop-workflows)`, a `/deep:*` command, or an agent.
    87	- **ALWAYS** keep `Skill(deep-loop-workflows)` hub routing, the `/deep:*` commands, and the agent types as complementary surfaces over the same packets — never let one surface fork per-mode logic out of its packet.
    88	
    89	### NEVER
    90	- **NEVER** add an `improvement` `loopType` to `deep-loop-runtime/convergence.cjs` (improvement is host-driven; `runtimeLoopType` stays `null`).
    91	- **NEVER** infer `runtimeLoopType` from `workflowMode` — read it from the registry (explicit `null` is load-bearing).
    92	- **NEVER** let a read-only mode (context/research/review/ai-council) reach the improvement mutation scripts (`promote-candidate.cjs`/`rollback-candidate.cjs`).
    93	- **NEVER** add a `graph-metadata.json` or a discoverable skill marker inside a mode packet or `shared/`.
    94	
    95	### ESCALATE IF
    96	- A new mode is needed beyond the eight registered — extend `mode-registry.json` and open a packet, do not bolt logic onto the hub.
    97	- A change would require the runtime to gain MCP tools or an improvement loopType — that contradicts the architecture; escalate.
    98	
    99	---
   100	
   101	## 5. REFERENCES
   102	
   103	- Backend: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/` (frozen, consumed by every mode).
   104	- Mode packets: `deep-context/SKILL.md`, `deep-research/SKILL.md`, `deep-review/SKILL.md`, `deep-ai-council/SKILL.md`, `deep-improvement/SKILL.md` (per-mode detail).
   105	- Commands: the eight `/deep:*` commands under `.opencode/commands/deep/` (complementary surface).
   106	- Registry: `mode-registry.json` (the routing contract — the authoritative `packet` paths).
   107	
   108	---
   109	
   110	## 6. SUCCESS CRITERIA
   111	
   112	- The hub resolves one primary `workflowMode` for the request through `mode-registry.json` (improvement folds to the right lane via the registry, never by array order).
   113	- The selected mode packet owns the detailed convergence/state/artifact workflow; the hub stayed routing-only.
   114	- `Skill(deep-loop-workflows[, hint])` reaches a mode, and the `/deep:*` commands and agent types still reach the same packets.
   115	- Exactly one `graph-metadata.json` exists for the whole skill; no packet carries its own.
   116	
   117	---
   118	
   119	## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS
   120	
   121	### Modes
   122	- `context` — inward codebase-context gathering, reuse-first Context Report.
   123	- `research` — outward web + code iterative investigation (`research/research.md`).
   124	- `review` — iterative code audit, P0/P1/P2 findings + verdict.
   125	- `ai-council` — multi-seat planning deliberation (`ai-council/**` artifacts).
   126	- `improvement` (4 lanes) — evaluator-first agent/model/skill/non-dev-system improvement.
   127	
   128	### Surfaces and Consumers
   129	- `Skill(deep-loop-workflows)` is the invokable hub; the `/deep:*` commands and the agent types (`deep-context`, `deep-research`, `deep-review`, `ai-council`, `deep-improvement`) are complementary surfaces over the same packets.
   130	- `deep-loop-runtime` is the frozen, MCP-free backend every mode consumes.
   131	- `/speckit:plan` consumes the context mode's Context Report; spec-folder docs consume research/review output.
   132	
   133	---
   134	
   135	## 8. RELATED RESOURCES
   136	
   137	- Pattern: `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md` (parent-skill hub + nested packets, the one-graph-metadata invariant).
   138	- Sibling example: `.opencode/skills/sk-design/` (the same invokable-hub + `mode-registry.json` Option E pattern).
   139	- Registry: `mode-registry.json` (this hub's routing contract).

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/SKILL.md:431:- ✅ Council requests route to the `deep-ai-council` advisor/packet surface (`packetSkillName` and `legacyAdvisorId` in `mode-registry.json`); `deep-ai-council` is the packet folder/SKILL.md name (folder == name), while the dispatched agent identity remains `ai-council`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:4:description: "Unified deep-loop workflow skill: routes a request to one of five modes (context, research, review, ai-council, improvement) over the shared deep-loop-runtime backend. Holds no per-mode logic — it dispatches by workflowMode through mode-registry.json. Use for codebase-context gathering, autonomous research, iterative code review, multi-seat AI Council planning, and evaluator-first agent/model/skill/non-dev-system improvement."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:8:<!-- Keywords: deep-loop-workflows, deep-loop, deep-context, deep-research, deep-review, deep-ai-council, deep-improvement, context-gathering, reuse-catalog, autoresearch, iterative-research, code-audit, severity-findings, P0-P1-P2, ai-council, council-deliberation, multi-seat-planning, agent-improvement, benchmark-harness, model-benchmark, skill-benchmark, non-dev-ai-system, convergence-detection, externalized-state, coverage-graph, mode-registry, workflowmode, runtimeloop-type, backendkind -->
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:12:One skill, five workflow modes, one shared backend. `deep-loop-workflows` is the public, advisor-routable home for every deep-loop persona; `deep-loop-runtime` is the frozen, MCP-free backend it consumes. This hub holds NO per-mode convergence, state, or synthesis logic — each mode keeps its own contract in its packet, and the hub only routes by `workflowMode` through `mode-registry.json`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:36:Routing is **registry-driven** (invokable-hub, Option E). `mode-registry.json` is the single source of truth; the hub reads it and does not re-derive the mapping. When invoked as `Skill(deep-loop-workflows[, "<mode>: <request>"])`, the hub classifies the request to a `workflowMode`, resolves it through the registry, and loads `registry[mode].packet`. The advisor routes any deep-loop query to the single identity `deep-loop-workflows`; the hub then picks the mode. The `/deep:*` commands and native agent types remain as complementary surfaces — they reach the same packets through static routers/agent definitions — and the hub holds NO per-mode logic.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:46:read mode-registry.json
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:48:  → load the mode packet at registry[mode].packet/SKILL.md
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:49:       e.g. registry["research"].packet → deep-loop-workflows/deep-research/SKILL.md
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:51:  → if registry[mode].runtimeLoopType !== null: backend = convergence.cjs --loop-type <runtimeLoopType>
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:55:Intent classification favors the single dominant deep-loop mode; a mode hint (`research: ...`, `review: ...`, `context: ...`, `ai-council: ...`, or an improvement lane) overrides the classifier. The legacy advisor projection maps stay hardcoded and drift-guarded against the registry, and the command files remain static routers with hardcoded asset/mode routing; neither resolves from `mode-registry.json` at runtime, but both stay equal to its projection.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:67:  mode-registry.json     # the three-tier discriminator + advisorRouting (single source of truth)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:73:Each mode packet is the former skill's content moved verbatim (its own `SKILL.md`, `references/`, `scripts/`, `assets/`, `feature_catalog/`, `manual_testing_playbook/`), with internal paths repointed and **no per-packet `graph-metadata.json`** — only this hub carries one, so the advisor discovers exactly one skill. The `deep-ai-council` packet folder follows the standard `folder == packetSkillName` convention (`deep-ai-council`); its legacy public surfaces (the `/deep:ai-council` command and the `ai-council` agent) intentionally keep the shorter `ai-council` key, so always resolve the packet path through `mode-registry.json` rather than hardcoding it.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:83:- **ALWAYS** resolve a mode through `mode-registry.json` (read the `packet` key; never hardcode a router mapping or packet path in the hub).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:84:- **ALWAYS** keep advisor projection maps hardcoded and drift-guarded against the registry; command mode routing is still hardcoded in the command files and does not resolve through `mode-registry.json`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:96:- A new mode is needed beyond the eight registered — extend `mode-registry.json` and open a packet, do not bolt logic onto the hub.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:106:- Registry: `mode-registry.json` (the routing contract — the authoritative `packet` paths).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:112:- The hub resolves one primary `workflowMode` for the request through `mode-registry.json` (improvement folds to the right lane via the registry, never by array order).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:138:- Sibling example: `.opencode/skills/sk-design/` (the same invokable-hub + `mode-registry.json` Option E pattern).

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:55:`deep-loop-workflows` was the canonical parent-skill example, and the alignment is now closed against the required R5 evidence. R1 is done: the hub documents Option E invokable-hub routing through `Skill(deep-loop-workflows)`, with `mode-registry.json` as the routing source of truth. R2 is done: the nested packet folder is `deep-ai-council`, matching the packet name, while `/deep:ai-council` and the `ai-council` agent remain legacy public surfaces. R3 is done as keep-all earned: all five mode catalogs are substantial and stay. R4 is done as keep: maintainer sign-off plus a green drift-guard keeps the merged-identity layer. NFR-S01 is resolved as an accepted hub union-grant permission contract. R5 is done: strict recursive spec validation, family package checks, routing fixtures, parent-skill invariants, and runtime-registration reachability are green. A full live-loop e2e was deliberately not run and remains optional.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json:71:      "agent": "ai-council",
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json:72:      "artifactRoot": "ai-council/",
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json:73:      "aliases": ["deep-ai-council", "ai council deliberation", "multi-seat planning council", "council convergence", "planning council"],
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json:76:        "legacyAdvisorId": "deep-ai-council",
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json:77:        "legacyAliases": ["@deep-ai-council", "deep-ai-council", "deep ai council", "ai council", "planning council", "council deliberation", "multi-ai-council"],

.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:2:name: ai-council
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:3:description: AI Council scoped-write planning architect: diverse AI lenses, multi-round deliberation, ai-council artifact writes only.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:25:# The AI Council: Multi-Strategy Planning Architect
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:27:The Multi-AI Council is a scoped-write planning architect that seeks diverse AI vantage points, distinct reasoning strategies, and multi-round deliberation before recommending a plan. It writes and edits only packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts, never runs shell commands, never patches files, and never mutates code or spec docs outside that artifact subtree.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:29:**CRITICAL**: You MUST seek diversity in both reasoning lens and AI vantage point. Do not run the same strategy three ways. Each council seat MUST contribute a distinct perspective, such as analytical decomposition, failure analysis, implementation pragmatism, architectural fit, external research, or consensus critique. Output is a plan plus packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts. NEVER write outside `ai-council/**`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:37:Single-round council behavior remains the default for this agent. Iterative multi-topic deep mode is available through `/deep:ai-council`, which wraps the council in session -> topic -> round state, cost guards, and adjudicator-verdict stability checks; see `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/SKILL.md` Section "Deep Mode (Iterative Multi-Topic)".
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:43:**Semantic:** the deep-ai-council threshold scores per-topic Round-N -> Round-N+1 verdict deltas from the adjudicator. Lower = more rounds / higher stability threshold.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:55:The Multi-AI Council uses **adaptive dispatch** based on invocation depth:
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:57:- **Depth 0** (invoked directly by user): Dispatch council seats via Task tool in parallel when available. Each seat must use a distinct strategy lens and, when the runtime supports it, a distinct AI-system vantage.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:58:- **Depth 1** (dispatched by orchestrator or another agent): Use `sequential_thinking` MCP inline, without sub-dispatch. Process each council seat sequentially within a single context. NDP compliant.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:64:**No recursive counciling**:
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:65:- Never dispatch the Multi-AI Council from inside itself.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:66:- Never ask a council seat to invoke another council.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:73:### 9-Step Multi-AI Council Process
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:75:0. **RESOLVE** -> Determine the target packet path BEFORE any seat dispatch. Persistence under `<packet>/ai-council/**` is mandatory; the packet path must be known before DIVERSIFY. Apply this resolution rule in order, stopping at the first match:
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:79:   4. Else, HALT and ask the user a single, focused question: "Which spec folder should I persist `ai-council/` artifacts under?" List the resolution candidates you tried in (1)-(3). Do NOT dispatch council seats. Do NOT emit a chat-form Council Report. Wait for the user to provide a packet path.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:81:   **Step 0 fails closed.** If none of (1)-(3) yield a packet path AND the user does not provide one when asked, the council MUST NOT proceed to DIVERSIFY. The HALT-and-ASK branch is the agent's only output for that turn.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:85:3. **DIVERSIFY** -> Select 2-3 council seats from distinct strategy lenses and AI vantage targets. The goal is principled disagreement and complementary coverage, not more copies of the same answer.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:86:4. **DISPATCH** -> Launch selected council seats in parallel (Depth 0) or process sequentially via `sequential_thinking` (Depth 1). Each seat receives the same task and evidence but a distinct mandate.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:93:8. **DELIVER** -> Present the Multi-AI Council Report with council composition, comparison table, deliberation notes, implementation roadmap, confidence score, and persisted `ai-council/**` artifacts. Do not apply code or spec changes outside the council artifact subtree.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:95:**Key Principle**: Deliberated consensus beats repeated intuition. The Multi-AI Council seeks varied reasoning, varied AI vantage points, and adversarial critique before recommending a plan. It analyzes, scores, and recommends; the user or another agent executes.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:115:| `@context` | Codebase context retrieval | Preparing accurate file, symbol, or packet context before council deliberation |
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:122:| `Task` | Dispatch council seats | Depth 0: parallel strategy execution |
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:134:> **Scoped-write permissions**: This agent has read/search access for analysis and may write/edit only packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:135:> Bash and Patch remain denied. Any write outside `ai-council/**` is an `OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE` violation.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:155:The council should seek distinct AI vantage points when available. Do not claim an external AI system participated unless the runtime actually dispatched it or an orchestrator provided its result.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:165:Every council run MUST satisfy all applicable diversity checks:
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:218:Never increase N above 3 to simulate consensus. If more than three vantage points matter, stage them in the plan as follow-up validation instead of dispatching an oversized council.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:226:When dispatching at Depth 0, use this template for each council seat:
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:229:You are the <STRATEGY_NAME> Council Seat for a Multi-AI Council planning run.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:284:- Launch all selected council seats simultaneously via `Task` tool when allowed.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:287:- **Use when**: Multi-AI Council is invoked directly at top level.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:291:- Use `sequential_thinking` MCP to process each council seat in order.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:294:- **Use when**: Multi-AI Council is dispatched by another agent, such as orchestrator.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:302:    │   └─► Process council seats inline, no Task dispatch
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:338:1. **Score each council seat** using the rubric above and produce a comparison table.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:350:**Purpose:** Counter convergence bias and shallow consensus. The council should seek genuine disagreement before concluding that a plan is strong.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:369:- Ask: "Are these genuinely the same good idea, or did the council fail to diversify?"
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:389:- Dispatch at least 2 distinct council seats (never a single approach).
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:393:- Include the comparison table in the Multi-AI Council Report.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:395:- Cite which council seat contributed each element of the final plan.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:398:- Graph updates belong to caller-owned `deep-loop-runtime` CLI reducers, not council-seat deliberation.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:399:- **Persist `ai-council/**` artifacts directly via the `lib/persist-artifacts.cjs` named exports BEFORE claiming completion.** The minimum required artifact set is: `ai-council-config.json`, `ai-council-state.jsonl` (with at least `round_start` + `seat_returned` x N + `deliberation_synthesized` + `round_end` + `council_complete` events), `ai-council-strategy.md`, `seats/round-NNN/seat-MMM-*.md` for each dispatched seat, `deliberations/round-NNN.md`, and `council-report.md`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:403:- Modify files outside `ai-council/**`. This is a scoped-write planning agent; code/spec implementation belongs to the user or another agent.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:404:- **Deliver a council report without persisting the canonical artifact set.** A chat-only response without `ai-council/**` artifacts on disk is an enforcement violation, not a successful run.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:406:- Use Bash or Patch tools, or write/edit outside `ai-council/**`. Bash and Patch stay denied; out-of-scope writes must fail with `OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:407:- Run identical council seats or rephrased duplicates.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:409:- Dispatch more than 3 seats in one council run.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:411:- Nest Multi-AI Council within Multi-AI Council.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:412:- Claim multi-AI consensus when the council used only one vantage or one repeated lens.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:416:- All council seats score below 50. Task may need reframing.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:418:- A council seat fails or times out and N drops below 2.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:426:The canonical schema for §8 lives at `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/output_schema.md` — both this section and the `persist-artifacts.cjs` helper cite it. Schema changes require lockstep update of all three.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:428:### Multi-AI Council Report
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:431:## Multi-AI Council Report: <task summary>
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:494:- No files were modified by the Multi-AI Council.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:508:[] At least 2 distinct council seats returned results (or timeouts noted)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:524:[] No file modifications attempted OUTSIDE `ai-council/**` (scoped-write only; writes to `<packet>/ai-council/**` are mandatory and tracked separately in PERSISTENCE VERIFICATION below)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:527:[] All claims have citations (file:line OR council seat source)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:534:[] `<packet>/ai-council/` directory exists at the resolved target path.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:535:[] `ai-council-config.json` written via `writeConfig` with current_round and status.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:536:[] `ai-council-state.jsonl` contains at minimum `round_start`, `seat_returned` (one per dispatched seat), `deliberation_synthesized`, `round_end`, and `council_complete` events.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:537:[] `ai-council-strategy.md` written via `writeStrategyMd` with charter content.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:540:[] `council-report.md` written via `writeReport` with the final synthesized plan.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:550:1. Did I dispatch or process at least 2 distinct council seats? (YES/NO)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:559:10. Did I avoid file modifications outside `ai-council/**` (i.e., scoped-write only)? (YES/NO)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:560:11. Did I persist the canonical `ai-council/**` artifact set, ending with `council-report.md` and a `council_complete` state event? (YES/NO)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:563:Fix verification gaps first. For Q11 specifically: re-run the missing writer calls and confirm the artifact_written events landed in `ai-council-state.jsonl` before delivering.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:576:- If a council seat does not return within the expected time:
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:585:  - Report: "All council seats failed. Task may need reframing."
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:595:  - Include: "ESCALATION: Contradictory high-confidence council recommendations require user decision."
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:615:| **Out-of-scope File Modification** | Council write authority is limited to `ai-council/**` | Persist council artifacts only. User or another agent executes code/spec changes |
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:617:| **Recursive Council** | Nesting the council inside itself creates infinite loops | Multi-AI Council is a planning leaf, no self-recursion |
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:624:## 12. OUTPUT PROTOCOL - `ai-council/` SUBFOLDER CONVENTION
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:626:Persist council artifacts under `<packet>/ai-council/` where `<packet>` is the path resolved at §1 Step 0 RESOLVE. Persistence is mandatory on every council run; the prior conditional language ("when invoked with a spec_folder") was removed because Step 0 ensures the packet path is always available before DELIVER. If Step 0 cannot resolve a packet path and the user is not present to answer the HALT-and-ASK question, the council does NOT run — the layout below is therefore guaranteed to apply to every completed deliberation. Layout parity with `research/` from deep-research and `review/` from deep-review. Chat output may summarize the result, but the packet artifact is the source of truth for rounds, seats, critique, resume, and final plan.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:629:specs/<track>/<NNN-name>/ai-council/
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:630:|-- ai-council-config.json     (one per packet, mutated across runs)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:631:|-- ai-council-strategy.md     (charter authored on first run)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:632:|-- ai-council-state.jsonl     (append-only state log)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:636:|-- council-report.md          (FINAL synthesized plan)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:640:- `ai-council-config.json`: `{spec_folder, current_round, max_rounds, seats_per_round, convergence_signal, created_at, updated_at, status}`. Mutate across runs; do not duplicate.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:641:- `ai-council-strategy.md`: charter with purpose, task framing, selected lenses, executor/vantage targets, evidence inputs, convergence rule, and known constraints.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:642:- `ai-council-state.jsonl`: append-only events: `round_start`, `seat_returned`, `deliberation_synthesized`, `round_end`, `council_complete`, plus v1.2 `artifact_written`, `rollback`, and `artifact_superseded`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:646:- `council-report.md`: final synthesized plan with composition, comparison, recommended roadmap, rejected alternatives, risks, confidence, and convergence status.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:648:Reference: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:654:1. **First call** (no `ai-council/` folder exists at the resolved packet path): execute these writer calls in order. Each persistence step MUST emit an `artifact_written` event in `ai-council-state.jsonl` before the next step begins. Treat any skipped step as a §9 Q11 violation.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:655:   1. `writeConfig(...)` -> initialize `ai-council-config.json` with `current_round: 1`, `status: "in_progress"`, and timestamps. (emit `artifact_written`)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:658:   4. Dispatch council seats — parallel via `Task` at Depth 0, sequential via `sequential_thinking` at Depth 1. (no artifact_written here; dispatch is in-memory)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:663:   9. `writeReport(...)` -> write the final `council-report.md`. (emit `artifact_written`)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:664:   10. `writeStateJsonl({ event: "council_complete", final_report_path: "<path>", convergence: true|false })` -> close the run. (emit `artifact_written`)
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:665:2. **Subsequent call** (the `ai-council/` folder already exists at the resolved path): read `ai-council-config.json` and `ai-council-state.jsonl`. Determine the next round from `(highest round_end event).round + 1`. Run new seats with prior deliberation as input, then follow steps 5-10 of the first-call sequence with the new round number. Append state events; do not rewrite history.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:668:Reference: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:674:Schema is convention-only for v1 under the council state contract; there is no runtime validator.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:681:type CouncilComplete = {event:"council_complete"; timestamp:string; final_report_path:string; convergence?:boolean};
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:692:{"event":"council_complete","timestamp":"<ISO>","final_report_path":"<path>"}
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:695:`ai-council-state.jsonl` is append-only. NEVER rewrite or truncate it. NEVER write secrets, credentials, tokens, or private keys to state events.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:699:Writer-emitted rows may prefix each event with `schema_version`, `protocol`, and `producer`. Missing `schema_version` means implicit `"1"`; v1.2 writers emit `"1.2"`, `protocol:"ai-council"`, and `producer:"persist-artifacts@1.2.0"`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:701:Evolution is additive-only: v1 callers must keep working, and old rows are not rewritten. Full state-format rules live in `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/state_format.md`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:707:Default signal: `two-of-three-agree`. If 2 of 3 seats endorse essentially the same plan and the cross-critique round produces no new high-severity findings, declare convergence and write `council-report.md`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:710:- `max_rounds` reached without convergence: emit `council_complete` with `convergence:false` and recommend user decision.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:720:The council writes packet artifacts directly through `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/lib/persist-artifacts.cjs`. Use the named exports in order as each round closes: `writeStateJsonl`, `writeConfig`, `writeStrategyMd`, `writeSeat`, `writeDeliberation`, `writeCritique`, and `writeReport`. Each writer resolves the target under `<packet>/ai-council/`, writes the artifact, then appends an `artifact_written` event with byte count and sha256 checksum to `ai-council-state.jsonl`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:724:1. **Allowed root**: `<packet>/ai-council/**` only.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:726:3. **Out-of-scope writes**: any target outside `ai-council/**` must fail with `OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE` before touching the filesystem.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:727:4. **Helper fallback**: non-council callers may still invoke `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs`. The helper is now a thin CLI wrapper around the same library exports.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:729:Depth-1 rule: the LEAF council owns writes to `ai-council/**` directly. The dispatching parent owns code/spec implementation after the council returns, but it does not need to invoke the helper for normal council artifact persistence.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:731:Forward-only scope: this convention applies to council dispatches from this point forward. Pre-080 outputs in earlier packets remain in their original locations and are not migrated retroactively.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:735:Fallback callers may add `--memory-save-payload-out FILE` when invoking the helper. On `council_complete`, the helper writes a `generate-context.js` compatible JSON payload; without the flag, no payload is written.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:738:node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs <packet> \
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:739:  --input-file council-report.md \
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:740:  --memory-save-payload-out /tmp/council-payload.json
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:743:  /tmp/council-payload.json <packet>
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:746:The payload routes through existing decision-record, implementation-summary, and handover categories. No new ANCHOR family is introduced. See `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md`.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:752:Round rollback is scoped to one `round-NNN` unit. If a seat errors, times out below the minimum quorum, or convergence fails after the configured maximum, write a `rollback` event to `ai-council-state.jsonl`, move the round artifacts into `ai-council/failed/round-NNN-<timestamp>/`, and append `artifact_superseded` markers for every `artifact_written` event from that round.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:756:1. Inspect `ai-council/failed/round-NNN-<timestamp>/` for forensic context.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:758:3. Start a fresh council dispatch for the next round or ask the orchestrator to reframe the task. In-place retry is out of scope.
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:772:│  ├─► Dispatch 2-3 distinct council seats with unique strategy lenses    │
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:776:│  └─► Output plan plus scoped ai-council artifacts                         │
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:786:│  └─► 8. DELIVER     Report + ai-council artifacts                         │
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:789:│  ├─► Multi-AI Council Report (composition + comparison table)           │
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:794:│  ├─► Max 3 council seats per task                                       │
.opencode/agents/ai-council.md:795:│  ├─► Writes/edits only within ai-council/**                              │

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_confirm.yaml:3:purpose: Multi-topic council deliberation with user approval gates, findings registry, and cross-topic priors
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_confirm.yaml:4:action: Run deep-ai-council session orchestration with confirmation before setup, loop, synthesis, and save

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_confirm.yaml:44:    findings_registry: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/lib/findings-registry.cjs

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_confirm.yaml:52:    graph_convergence: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs --loop-type council

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_confirm.yaml:57:  session_config: "{spec_folder}/ai-council/council-session.json"

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:1:# Deep Ask AI Council Presentation Contract
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:3:Presentation source of truth for `/deep:ai-council`. The command router owns only mode selection, safety gates, and asset loading. Workflow YAML owns execution. This file owns visible prompts, dashboard/checkpoint layout, result displays, examples, and next-step suggestions.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:19:1. **Tier 1 - Resolve confidently**: parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags, the `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block, and the Default Resolution Table below. When every required field is resolved, persist to `{spec_folder}/ai-council/council-session.json` or the YAML-designated setup artifact, bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, and load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_auto.yaml`. End Section 0.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:21:3. **Tier 3 - Fail fast**: emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:ai-council:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:121:   - memory_context({ input: deliberation_topic OR "deep-ai-council", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:126:   Q0. Deliberation Topic or Topics (if not in command): What should the council deliberate?
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:144:     A) Active runtime / in-cli (default) - use the current runtime's council seats.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:152:   - `"Deep council architecture, D, A, rounds=3 topics=5 saturation=0.20 convergence=0.20, A"`
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:191:Run a bounded multi-topic AI Council session: initialize session state, deliberate topic-by-topic, evaluate adjudicator-verdict stability, synthesize per-topic and session-wide reports, and route continuity through the spec packet.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:206:role: Deep AI Council Session Manager
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:207:purpose: Run iterative multi-topic council sessions until per-topic convergence or cost guards stop the run
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:218:Run an iterative multi-topic council session under `{spec_folder}/ai-council/`: initialize `council-session.json`, run topic loops with session-wide findings registry priors, compile per-topic reports, compile `session-report.md`, and refresh packet continuity.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:220:Use this when a planning problem needs more than one council topic, more than one round per topic, or explicit cost/convergence guards. For single-round planning, use the regular `ai-council` agent behavior.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:226:**Outputs:** Packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts, including session config/state, topic folders, per-topic council reports, session report, findings registry, and `STATUS=<OK|FAIL|CANCELLED>`.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:232:| Setup | Initialize | Resolve setup answers, cost guards, executor, spec folder, and session state | `ai-council/council-session.json`, session directories |
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:233:| Loop | Iterate | Run bounded topic loops and evaluate adjudicator-verdict stability | `ai-council/topics/**`, session state, findings registry |
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:234:| Synth | Synthesize | Build per-topic reports and a session-wide report with cross-topic priors | `topic-report.md`, `council-report.md`, `session-report.md` |
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:241:| `:auto` | `/deep:ai-council:auto "topic"` | Runs setup, loop, synthesis, and save without approval gates after Tier-1 setup succeeds |
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:242:| `:confirm` | `/deep:ai-council:confirm "topic"` | Prompts once for setup, then gates setup, loop, synthesis, and save |
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt:243:| (default) | `/deep:ai-council "topic"` | Ask user to choose mode during setup |

.opencode/commands/deep/review.md:113:The YAML workflow runs iterative code review by initializing a review packet under `{artifact_dir}`, dispatching fresh `@deep-review` LEAF agents, evaluating convergence across configured review dimensions, and synthesizing findings into `review-report.md` plus optional `resource-map.md`. Convergence uses the review-specific `convergenceThreshold` severity-weighted finding ratio; do not transfer sibling defaults from deep-research or deep-ai-council. Code graph tool IDs remain stable as `code_graph_query` and `code_graph_context`; implementation and docs live under `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/`.

.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:3:description: Multi-topic deep-ai-council session loop with adjudicator-verdict stability. Modes :auto, :confirm.
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:10:Thin router for the deep-ai-council session loop. This command verifies the orchestrating agent, resolves setup and execution mode, loads the presentation contract, then executes the owned workflow YAML.
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:30:│   ├─ You can orchestrate the deep-ai-council session (YAML workflow execution)
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:45:    │   │ This command orchestrates the deep-ai-council session and  │
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:49:    │   │   /deep:ai-council [arguments]                             │
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:72:- **PLANNING BOUNDARY**: deep council writes packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts only. Implementation remains with the caller or follow-on implementation agents.
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:73:- **GRAPH BOUNDARY**: derived council graph replay uses `deep-loop-runtime` CLI scripts with `--loop-type council`; `ai-council/**` artifacts remain authoritative.
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:76:> **Canonical mode syntax:** use attached command suffixes (`/deep:ai-council:auto`, `/deep:ai-council:confirm`) and keep AGENTS, skills, command references, and runtime mirrors synchronized to this entrypoint.
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:84:| Presentation source of truth | `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt` |
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:85:| Auto workflow | `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_auto.yaml` |
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:86:| Confirm workflow | `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_confirm.yaml` |
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:94:1. Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for attached command suffixes (`:auto` or `:confirm`). Canonical mode syntax is `/deep:ai-council:auto` and `/deep:ai-council:confirm`.
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:107:| `:auto` | `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_auto.yaml` |
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:108:| `:confirm` or interactive choice | `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_confirm.yaml` |
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:114:The following content lives only in `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_ai-council_presentation.txt`:
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:118:- Dashboard/checkpoint layouts, workflow overview displays, mode behavior descriptions, and council reference displays.
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:126:The YAML workflow runs a bounded multi-topic AI Council session under `{spec_folder}/ai-council/`: it initializes session state, runs topic loops with findings-registry priors, evaluates adjudicator-verdict stability, synthesizes per-topic and session reports, and refreshes packet continuity. Convergence uses the council-specific 0.20 default on adjudicator-verdict stability; do not transfer sibling defaults from deep-review or deep-research. Packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts remain canonical, and derived council graph replay uses `deep-loop-runtime` CLI scripts with `--loop-type council`.
.opencode/commands/deep/ai-council.md:128:For single-round planning, use the regular `ai-council` agent behavior. After a successful deep council session, continue to implementation planning or the next packet phase.
.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/changelog/v0.3.0.md:1:This release lands the system-skill-advisor portion of the packets 108 plus 110 auto-review uplift program. Four reusable teachings from the upstream `dzianisv/opencode-plugins` auto-review package now apply to both the skill internals and the `mk-skill-advisor.js` plugin: async file I/O with a closure-scoped lazy-mkdir guard, opt-in debug logging, a 4-tier configuration precedence and awaited config loading at module init. Every mechanism cleared a 5-of-5 unanimous deep-ai-council review by `cli-codex gpt-5.5 xhigh fast` before any code shipped and the council reshaped the H-3 plus H-6 scope in a load-bearing way: the disk writes had to remain AWAITED rather than fire-and-forget so the bounded-retention contract for metrics records survives.
.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/changelog/v0.3.0.md:11:The advisor's metrics persistence in `mcp_server/lib/metrics.ts` used synchronous `mkdirSync` plus `writeFileSync` to flush diagnostic and outcome records, which blocked the event loop on every advisor invocation. The hot path is now `await mkdir` plus `await writeFile` from `fs/promises`, gated by a closure-scoped `dirReady = false` flag that ensures the directory check runs exactly once per process. The two persistence entrypoints (`persistAdvisorHookDiagnosticRecord` and `persistAdvisorHookOutcomeRecord`) are now async functions that await the underlying writes. Bounded-retention semantics, read the file, slice the last N records, rewrite the file, are preserved per the council's section 10.1 mandate, because making the writes fire-and-forget would have allowed unbounded growth between rotations.
.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/changelog/v0.3.0.md:29:The 4-tier config precedence is loaded as a `configPromise` at module init. The plugin factory now awaits that promise before option normalization, so configuration is fully resolved before the plugin reports its option surface to OpenCode. The council's section 10.2 review flagged the original race-prone path where a synchronous factory could return before the config file finished loading. That race no longer exists.
.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/changelog/v0.3.0.md:35:Both packets ran through a 5-seat deep-ai-council deliberation before any source change. The council returned REQUEST-CHANGES on the first pass with HIGH confidence and reshaped scope on three load-bearing points. First, the `metrics.ts` writes had to remain awaited so the bounded-retention read-then-rewrite-last-N pattern survives. Second, the plugin factory had to await the config promise before normalizing options. Third, the existing env-var disable handling at lines 35 through 39 had to be preserved unchanged so the disable surface remained stable. Implementation followed via `cli-opencode + deepseek-v4-pro --pure`.
.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/changelog/v0.3.0.md:46:| `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/metrics.ts` | Lines 218 through 248 (per the council-revised contract): `mkdirSync` plus `writeFileSync` replaced with awaited `mkdir` plus `writeFile` from `fs/promises`. Closure-scoped `dirReady` flag introduced at module scope (line 154); `SKILL_ADVISOR_DEBUG=1` enable gate added for diagnostic records. Bounded-retention read-then-rewrite-last-N preserved at line 253. `persistAdvisorHookDiagnosticRecord` and `persistAdvisorHookOutcomeRecord` now async. Touched in 65e6f0479. |

.opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/skill/parent_skill_hub_template.md:94:Routing is **registry-driven**. `mode-registry.json` is the single source of truth; the hub, commands, and advisor all read it and none re-derive the mapping.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/README.md:36:`Skill(deep-loop-workflows)` loads the hub, and the hub routes by `workflowMode` through `mode-registry.json` to one of five mode packets. The hub holds no per-mode logic. Each packet keeps its own convergence math, state shape, artifacts and tool-permission guards. The shared backend, `deep-loop-runtime`, does the executor configuration, prompt-pack rendering, validation, atomic state, coverage graph and scoring.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/README.md:50:The hub resolves `workflowMode: review` to the `deep-review` packet and runs its convergence loop.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/README.md:58:Every mode is described once in `mode-registry.json` by a three-tier discriminator, and no router re-derives that mapping:
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/README.md:60:- **`workflowMode`** is the public key used by commands, the advisor and the registry, for example `research`, `ai-council` or `agent-improvement`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/README.md:61:- **`runtimeLoopType`** is the graph-backed convergence key for `deep-loop-runtime`, one of `research`, `review`, `council` or `context`. It is an explicit `null` for the four improvement lanes and is never guessed from `workflowMode`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/README.md:79:| `mode-registry.json` | The single source of truth for the three-tier discriminator. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/README.md:102:| [`mode-registry.json`](./mode-registry.json) | The three-tier discriminator for every mode. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:4:description: "Unified deep-loop workflow skill: routes a request to one of five modes (context, research, review, ai-council, improvement) over the shared deep-loop-runtime backend. Holds no per-mode logic — it dispatches by workflowMode through mode-registry.json. Use for codebase-context gathering, autonomous research, iterative code review, multi-seat AI Council planning, and evaluator-first agent/model/skill/non-dev-system improvement."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:8:<!-- Keywords: deep-loop-workflows, deep-loop, deep-context, deep-research, deep-review, deep-ai-council, deep-improvement, context-gathering, reuse-catalog, autoresearch, iterative-research, code-audit, severity-findings, P0-P1-P2, ai-council, council-deliberation, multi-seat-planning, agent-improvement, benchmark-harness, model-benchmark, skill-benchmark, non-dev-ai-system, convergence-detection, externalized-state, coverage-graph, mode-registry, workflowmode, runtimeloop-type, backendkind -->
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:12:One skill, five workflow modes, one shared backend. `deep-loop-workflows` is the public, advisor-routable home for every deep-loop persona; `deep-loop-runtime` is the frozen, MCP-free backend it consumes. This hub holds NO per-mode convergence, state, or synthesis logic — each mode keeps its own contract in its packet, and the hub only routes by `workflowMode` through `mode-registry.json`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:18:Use this skill (through the hub) for any deep-loop workflow. Invoke it as `Skill(deep-loop-workflows)` (optionally with a mode hint such as `research: <request>`); the hub classifies the request, resolves a `workflowMode`, and loads the matching nested mode packet. The `/deep:*` commands and native agent types remain as complementary surfaces over the same packets.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:36:Routing is **registry-driven** (invokable-hub, Option E). `mode-registry.json` is the single source of truth; the hub reads it and does not re-derive the mapping. When invoked as `Skill(deep-loop-workflows[, "<mode>: <request>"])`, the hub classifies the request to a `workflowMode`, resolves it through the registry, and loads `registry[mode].packet`. The advisor routes any deep-loop query to the single identity `deep-loop-workflows`; the hub then picks the mode. The `/deep:*` commands and native agent types remain as complementary surfaces — they reach the same packets through static routers/agent definitions — and the hub holds NO per-mode logic.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:39:- **`workflowMode`** — the public mode key (all modes): `context`, `research`, `review`, `ai-council`, and the four improvement lanes `agent-improvement`, `model-benchmark`, `skill-benchmark`, `ai-system-improvement` (its loop-host mode stays `non-dev-ai-system-refine`).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:40:- **`runtimeLoopType`** — the graph-backed convergence key consumed by `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs` (validated against exactly `research|review|council|context`). **Explicit `null` for all four improvement lanes; never inferred from `workflowMode`.** Note `ai-council` maps to `runtimeLoopType: council`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:45:classify the request to a workflowMode (dominant deep-loop intent; mode hint like "research: ..." overrides)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:46:read mode-registry.json
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:47:  → resolve workflowMode from the hint / classified intent (or the /deep:* command / advisor alias)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:48:  → load the mode packet at registry[mode].packet/SKILL.md
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:51:  → if registry[mode].runtimeLoopType !== null: backend = convergence.cjs --loop-type <runtimeLoopType>
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:55:Intent classification favors the single dominant deep-loop mode; a mode hint (`research: ...`, `review: ...`, `context: ...`, `ai-council: ...`, or an improvement lane) overrides the classifier. The legacy advisor projection maps stay hardcoded and drift-guarded against the registry, and the command files remain static routers with hardcoded asset/mode routing; neither resolves from `mode-registry.json` at runtime, but both stay equal to its projection.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:67:  mode-registry.json     # the three-tier discriminator + advisorRouting (single source of truth)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:73:Each mode packet is the former skill's content moved verbatim (its own `SKILL.md`, `references/`, `scripts/`, `assets/`, `feature_catalog/`, `manual_testing_playbook/`), with internal paths repointed and **no per-packet `graph-metadata.json`** — only this hub carries one, so the advisor discovers exactly one skill. The `deep-ai-council` packet folder follows the standard `folder == packetSkillName` convention (`deep-ai-council`); its legacy public surfaces (the `/deep:ai-council` command and the `ai-council` agent) intentionally keep the shorter `ai-council` key, so always resolve the packet path through `mode-registry.json` rather than hardcoding it.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:83:- **ALWAYS** resolve a mode through `mode-registry.json` (read the `packet` key; never hardcode a router mapping or packet path in the hub).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:84:- **ALWAYS** keep advisor projection maps hardcoded and drift-guarded against the registry; command mode routing is still hardcoded in the command files and does not resolve through `mode-registry.json`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:91:- **NEVER** infer `runtimeLoopType` from `workflowMode` — read it from the registry (explicit `null` is load-bearing).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:96:- A new mode is needed beyond the eight registered — extend `mode-registry.json` and open a packet, do not bolt logic onto the hub.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:106:- Registry: `mode-registry.json` (the routing contract — the authoritative `packet` paths).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:112:- The hub resolves one primary `workflowMode` for the request through `mode-registry.json` (improvement folds to the right lane via the registry, never by array order).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:138:- Sibling example: `.opencode/skills/sk-design/` (the same invokable-hub + `mode-registry.json` Option E pattern).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:139:- Registry: `mode-registry.json` (this hub's routing contract).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/SKILL.md:431:- ✅ Council requests route to the `deep-ai-council` advisor/packet surface (`packetSkillName` and `legacyAdvisorId` in `mode-registry.json`); `deep-ai-council` is the packet folder/SKILL.md name (folder == name), while the dispatched agent identity remains `ai-council`.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:8:  - "advisorRouting routingClass"
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:23:This reference defines the parent-skill pattern in full: its anatomy, the single load-bearing invariant (exactly one `graph-metadata.json` per parent skill), the registry `advisorRouting` contract, the drift-guard rule that keeps hardcoded projection maps honest, and the naming standard. It is the most deep-linked skill-creation content because it governs how multi-mode skills stay discoverable without re-creating multi-identity brittleness.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:34:- [parent_skill_registry_template.json](../../assets/skill/parent_skill_registry_template.json) - mode-registry.json scaffold
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:45:Worked example throughout: [`.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/`](../../../deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md) (hub + `mode-registry.json` + five mode packets — four over the frozen `deep-loop-runtime` backend, the `deep-improvement` packet over `improvement-host`/`external-adapter` per its `backendKind`).
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:52:├── mode-registry.json     # declarative single source of truth (the discriminator + advisorRouting)
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:60:- **Thin hub `SKILL.md`** — routes by mode key through `mode-registry.json` and holds **no** per-mode convergence, state, or synthesis logic. Every mode keeps its own contract in its packet.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:61:- **Declarative `mode-registry.json`** — the single source of truth. Routers, commands, and tests **read** it; none re-derive the mapping. It carries the 3-tier discriminator plus the `advisorRouting` projection (below).
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:76:### The Registry `advisorRouting` Contract
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:78:Each mode in `mode-registry.json` carries an `advisorRouting` block describing **how the advisor routes to it**. A `routingClass` discriminates the four real routing mechanisms; the other fields supply the data the advisor's projection maps key on.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:81:"advisorRouting": {
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:95:| `alias-fold` | TS `DEEP_MODE_BY_CANONICAL` only — an alias projection (a multi-mode id folding to its default mode), not lexically scored. | Yes |
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:106:Keep the advisor's **hardcoded** projection maps (Python `DEEP_ROUTING_MODE_BY_KEY`, TypeScript `DEEP_MODE_BY_CANONICAL`). A CI test asserts those maps **equal** the registry's `advisorRouting` projection, and treats the registry as authoritative — a map change without a matching registry change fails CI.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:108:- **Do NOT make the advisor read `mode-registry.json` at runtime.** Runtime registry-loading buys nothing but hot-path I/O and a novel cross-skill import coupling (the advisor reaching into another skill's directory). The drift-guard gives the same anti-drift guarantee at test time, with zero runtime cost.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:110:- The guard lives at `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/tests/routing-registry-drift-guard.vitest.ts` for the worked example.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:116:- The advisor's merged-identity projection (`MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID` + `DEEP_MODE_BY_CANONICAL`) is a single global map. A second parent skill needs its **own** merged-identity layer and its **own** drift-guard test scoped to its `mode-registry.json`.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:122:- Resolve a mode through `mode-registry.json`; never hardcode a router mapping in the hub.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:125:- Give every mode an `advisorRouting` block with a valid `routingClass` and `packetSkillName`.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:130:- Make the advisor read `mode-registry.json` at runtime — keep the projection maps in code and guard them with the test.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:142:- Registry scaffold: [parent_skill_registry_template.json](../../assets/skill/parent_skill_registry_template.json) - `mode-registry.json` with the 3-tier discriminator + one example mode per `routingClass`.
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:143:- Canonical example: [`deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md`](../../../deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md), [`mode-registry.json`](../../../deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json), [`graph-metadata.json`](../../../deep-loop-workflows/graph-metadata.json).
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md:156:- [parent_skill_registry_template.json](../../assets/skill/parent_skill_registry_template.json) - Parent-skill mode-registry.json
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation.md:48:| **Parent skills with nested mode packets** — one hub dispatching to self-contained mode packets over a shared backend, the single `graph-metadata.json` invariant, the registry `advisorRouting` contract | [parent_skills_nested_packets.md](./skill_creation/parent_skills_nested_packets.md) | Designing or repairing a multi-mode parent skill |
.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation.md:68:- [parent_skill_registry_template.json](../assets/skill/parent_skill_registry_template.json) - Parent-skill mode-registry.json
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.4.0.0.md:19:The packet path moves from `deep-loop-workflows/ai-council/` to `deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/`. What stays the same: the `workflowMode` key, the `/deep:ai-council` command, the `@ai-council` agent and the `<packet>/ai-council/` artifact root. Only the skill packet path changed, so no command, agent or stored-artifact path breaks.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/graph-metadata.json:156:      "mode-registry.json",
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json:4:  "description": "Declarative mode registry: the single source of truth for the three-tier discriminator (workflowMode/runtimeLoopType/backendKind) PLUS the advisorRouting projection. .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json:11:    "routingClass": "How the advisor finds the mode. .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:19:The hub SKILL.md now carries the smart-routing contract, so an invocation of the parent loads the hub and routes to one mode packet. The five mode packets are `deep-research`, `deep-review`, `deep-context`, `deep-ai-council` and `deep-improvement`, each registered in `mode-registry.json` and each holding its own loop logic behind the hub's single `graph-metadata.json` identity.

.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:3:description: "Distinctive, intentional UI design and the full design surface: visual direction, taste, and build for interfaces; color, typography, layout, spacing, hierarchy, and design tokens; animation, transitions, and micro-interactions; accessibility, performance, responsive, theming, and anti-slop design audit with quality scoring; and live-website CSS to Style Reference DESIGN.md extraction. Use to make a UI look custom and polished rather than templated, design a visual system, choreograph motion, audit and harden design quality, or extract a real design system from a live site. The single advisor-routable design skill: it routes to five modes (interface, foundations, motion, audit, md-generator) via mode-registry.json, and each mode holds its own design logic."
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:11:<!-- Keywords: sk-design, design-family, mode-registry, workflowmode, backendkind, reference-base, anti-slop, design-tokens, cognitive-laws, interface-design, frontend-design, visual-design, visual-identity, make-it-look-good, looks-templated, redesign-the-ui, hero-section, ui-build, typography, palette, color-palette, font-pairing, ux-quality-checklist, design-variations, foundations, color-system, oklch, color-token-system, dark-mode, typography-scale, spacing-system, responsive-layout, layout, hierarchy, grid, themes, motion-design, animation, animate-this, transitions, micro-interactions, framer-motion, animatepresence, exit-animation, reduced-motion, morphing-icons, motion-performance, design-audit, ui-critique, accessibility-audit, performance-audit, anti-slop-detection, production-hardening, design-quality-score, P0-P1-design-findings, polish, theming, design.md, design-md, css-extraction, website-design-extraction, design-reference, tokens.json, playwright, design-to-markdown, design-system-generator, css-tokens, color-extraction, typography-extraction, hex-extraction, shadow-extraction, spacing-extraction, design-fidelity, anti-hallucination, extract-design-system, generate-design-md, capture-website-css, design-tokens-from-url, distinctive-interface, intentional-design, polished-ui, refined-ui, custom-not-templated, premium-ui, aesthetic, design-taste, visual-direction, craft, make-it-beautiful, less-generic, redesign-distinctive, smooth-animation, hover-effect, scroll-animation, choreography, interaction-feel, animate-the-menu, transition-design, design-audit, accessibility-audit, design-quality-audit, design-review, design-qa, audit-the-design, review-the-ui, wcag-contrast, ui-quality-review -->
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:15:One skill, five design modes, one shared reference base. `sk-design` is the public, advisor-routable home for every design persona; the shared design reference base (anti-slop principles, cognitive laws, design-token vocabulary) is the common vocabulary the modes cite. This hub holds NO per-mode design logic — each mode keeps its own contract in its packet, and the hub only routes by `workflowMode` through `mode-registry.json`.
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:41:Routing is **registry-driven**. `mode-registry.json` is the single source of truth; the hub reads it and does not re-derive the mapping. The advisor routes any design query to the single identity `sk-design`; the hub then picks the mode.
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:44:- **`workflowMode`** — the public mode key (every mode): `interface`, `foundations`, `motion`, `audit`, `md-generator`.
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:49:classify the request to a workflowMode (dominant design intent; mode hint like "motion: ..." overrides)
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:50:read mode-registry.json
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:51:  → resolve workflowMode from the hint / classified intent
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:52:  → load the mode packet at registry[mode].packet (e.g. sk-design/design-interface/SKILL.md)
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:53:  → the mode cites the shared reference base, or runs its own backend per registry[mode].backendKind
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:72:  mode-registry.json     # the discriminator + advisorRouting (single source of truth)
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:88:- **ALWAYS** resolve a mode through `mode-registry.json`; never hardcode a router mapping in the hub.
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:91:- **ALWAYS** give every mode an `advisorRouting` block with a valid `routingClass` and `packetSkillName`.
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:101:- A new design mode is needed — extend `mode-registry.json` and open a packet; do not bolt logic onto the hub.
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:111:- Registry: `mode-registry.json` (the routing contract).
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:144:- Canonical example: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/` (hub + `mode-registry.json` + mode packets).
.opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md:145:- Registry: `mode-registry.json` (this hub's routing contract).
.opencode/skills/sk-design/README.md:36:`Skill(sk-design)` loads the hub, and the hub routes the request to one of five modes through `mode-registry.json`. Each mode holds its own design logic and the hub itself is routing-only. sk-design owns the taste and the system. It hands the actual build to `sk-code` and uses `mcp-open-design` or `mcp-figma` only as transport.
.opencode/skills/sk-design/README.md:95:| [`mode-registry.json`](./mode-registry.json) | The mode-to-packet routing map. |
.opencode/skills/sk-design/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:15:- `mode-registry.json` mapping each workflow mode to its nested packet.

The four doc-guidance modes cite the shared reference base (anti-slop principles, cognitive laws, design-token vocabulary) for consistent vocabulary, while md-generator runs its own Playwright CSS-extraction backend (backendKind discriminates the two). The hub holds no per-mode design logic; every mode keeps its own judgment, examples, and verification in its packet, and all five route by hub membership so the advisor sees exactly one design identity.",

codex
The actual registry and advisor tests use a `modes` array, so the `registry[mode]` wording may be pseudocode rather than executable shape. I’m not going to count that unless it creates a concrete contradiction against the live contract.

    35	# Feature Specification: Parent-skill native invocability
    36	
    37	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->
    38	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: spec-core | v2.2 -->
    39	<!-- HVR_REFERENCE: .opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/global/hvr_rules.md -->
    40	
    41	---
    42	
    43	## Root Purpose
    44	
    45	Make a parent skill's nested mode packets natively reachable without breaking the one-`graph-metadata.json` single-identity invariant, then propagate the resulting parent-skill optimizations across the skill system. This is a **phase parent**: the mechanism decision and each per-family alignment live in phase children; this root carries only the shared purpose and the phase map.
    46	
    47	---
    48	
    49	## Phase Documentation Map
    50	
    51	| Phase | Purpose | Level | Status |
    52	|-------|---------|-------|--------|
    53	| **001-invocability-mechanism** | The invocability gap and the chosen mechanism: Option E, invokable-hub routing. `Skill(<parent>)` loads the hub (the one top-level identity), and the hub router loads the matching nested mode packet. | 3 | Decision complete: ADR-001 Accepted; no source build in 001; NFR-S01 carried to 002 |
    54	| **002-deep-loop-alignment** | Align the deep-loop parent-skill family (`deep-loop-workflows` + `deep-loop-runtime`) with the accepted mechanism: invokable-hub routing, `deep-ai-council` folder/name identity, feature-catalog hygiene, runtime reconciliation, and validation. | 3 | Complete: R1-R5 done; all required gates green; live-loop e2e optional/not run |
    55	
    56	---
    57	
    58	## Scope
    59	
    60	**In scope:** the invocability mechanism (001) and its propagation to the deep-loop parent-skill family (002).
    61	
    62	**Out of scope:** building net-new design sub-skills (that belongs to the 154 family's gap work); any runtime/binary change (Option D was rejected as out-of-repo); the advisor merged-identity layer except where 002 explicitly evaluates it for deep-loop routing strength.
    63	
    64	---
    65	
    66	## Success Criteria
    67	
    68	- A nested mode is reachable through its invokable parent hub, with exactly one advisor identity preserved (001).
    69	- The deep-loop parent-skill family either matches the sk-design parent-skill conventions, or each deliberate divergence is recorded with its rationale (002).
    70	- `validate.sh --recursive` passes for the parent and both phase children as a structural gate; semantic review findings close only when child docs and evidence explicitly satisfy them.
